general_exclusion_criteria: Active brain metastases or carcinomatous meningitis; unresolved ocular diseases such as glaucoma, retinal vascular occlusion risk factors, corneal epithelial instability, degenerations, keratitis, or other serious ocular surface inflammation; history of rhabdomyolysis or neuromuscular disorders associated with elevated creatine kinase; uncontrolled concurrent illnesses including uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, New York Heart Association class III or IV congestive heart failure, recent (< six months) myocardial infarction, symptomatic coronary artery disease, syncope of cardiac cause, sustained ventricular tachyarrhythmias, poorly controlled diabetes mellitus, moderate-to-severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, severe chronic liver disease, severe renal impairment, or any life‑threatening cardiac, pulmonary, hepatic, or renal condition; secondary malignant neoplasms unless disease free ≥ five years (exceptions permitted for adequately treated cervix carcinoma in situ or non‑melanoma skin cancer); hypersensitivity to study drug constituents or structurally similar substances; active Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection with an AIDS‑defining opportunistic infection within the past year or ongoing receipt of potent CYP3A4‑modulating antiretrovirals; active hepatitis B or C infection (unless appropriately suppressed or cured as stipulated); concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inductors; pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; inadequate contraception compliance; inability to ingest/swallow oral medication or conditions impairing gastrointestinal absorption (gastrectomy, extensive small‑bowel resections, active inflammatory bowel disease, baseline diarrhoea grade ≥ 2); active systemic bacterial or fungal infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy; SARS‑CoV‑2 infection within twenty‑eight days preceding study start; current warfarin therapy; any other serious or unmanaged medical condition (e.g., interstitial lung disease, resting hypoxemia requiring supplemental oxygen, significant neuropsychiatric disease) deeming the patient unsuitable for safe trial participation.

general_exclusion_criteria: Concurrent primary malignancy requiring active cytotoxic chemotherapy or radiotherapy.; Known inherited bone‑marrow failure disorder or germline predisposition to hematologic malignancy.; Diagnosis of myelodysplastic syndrome, myeloproliferative neoplasm, chronic myelomonocytic leukemia, acute myeloid leukemia, or any other hematolymphoid malignancy at any point in life.; Presence of smoldering multiple myeloma, smoldering Waldenströmʼs macroglobulinemia, monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance, or monoclonal B‑cell lymphocytosis.; Active uncontrolled systemic fungal, bacterial, or viral infection.; Recent administration (≤3 months) of a live attenuated vaccine or planning to receive one within the next 15 months.; Laboratory evidence of clinically significant red‑cell hemolysis.; Clinical or imaging findings suggestive of hypersplenism or portal hypertension.; Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

general_exclusion_criteria: Participants with distant metastasis outside the abdominopelvic cavity; recent (<3 months) bowel obstruction or high risk thereof; need for parenteral nutrition or IV fluid dependency; concomitant investigation agent administration; solid organ transplantation; actively progressing second malignant disease or other malignancy within 2 years (excluding specified low‑risk tumors); severe hypersensitivity to components resembling N‑803; prior immune‑checkpoint inhibition resulting in irreversible immune‑related toxicity or needing intense/prolonged immunosuppression (endocrinopathies controlled on hormone replacement permitted); significant autoimmune disorders—including inflammatory bowel disease, rheumatoid arthritis, systemic scleroderma, systemic lupus erythematosus, autoimmune vasculitis, Guillain‑Barré syndrome, Myasthenia Gravis (Hashimoto’s thyroiditis acceptable); chronic systemic glucocorticoids (>10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent for ≥4 weeks before infusion); uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, dangerous arrhythmia, or major psychiatric disorder impairing protocol adherence; pregnancy or breastfeeding; HIV‑positive status; untreated active hepatitis B or C; existing non‑ infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days preceding treatment; anaphylactic reaction to murine‑based antibodies or iron dextran; history of grade 2 or greater hemolytic anemia.

general_exclusion_criteria: Concurrent use of any other investigational or study agents for the malignant indication.; Prior treatment with a topoisomerase I‑targeting ADC not permitted apart from stipulated exceptions.; Live attenuated vaccination within 30 days prior to first dosing.; Clinically significant corneal disease.; Severe hypersensitivity reaction to trastuzumab deruxtecan, datopotamab deruxtecan, or related excipients.; Hypersensitivity to other monoclonal antibodies.; Major surgery within 2 weeks prior to initiating study treatment.; Uncontrolled intercurrent serious illness—including active infection, unmanaged non‑malignant systemic disease, uncontrolled seizures, or psychiatric/socioeconomic factors limiting compliance.; Current or unresolved non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease requiring steroids.; Significant preexisting pulmonary disorders (recent PE, severe asthma/COPD, restrictive disease, pleural effusions, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disease with lung involvement, prior pneumonectomy.).; Prolonged corrected QT interval (>470 ms women, >450 ms men).; Recent (<6 months) cardiovascular interventions or events (CABG, PCI, stenting, MI, unstable angina, NYHA class II⁺ heart failure, stroke).; Second primary malignancy unless disease free ≥3 years or meets predefined low‑risk criteria (certain in‑situ carcinomas, superficial bladder cancer, non‑melanoma skin cancer).; Untreated or poorly controlled HIV infection.; Active or untreated Hepatitis B or C infection.; Active infectious processes including tuberculosis.; Conditions, therapies, or lab abnormalities that could confound efficacy assessment or jeopardize safe completion of the study.; Pregnancy, lactation, or intent to conceive.

Substance abuse or other serious medical conditions increasing safety risks or interfering with protocol compliance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: head and neck cancer. Histology allowed: any. Cancer burden allowed: Grade 2 or Grade 3 radiation‑induced late xerostomia occurring at least 3 years after completion of beam radiation therapy; no evidence of recurrent or metastatic disease; no second primary cancer (exceptions: treated basal cell or cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, or in‑situ cervical carcinoma). Prior treatment required: Definitive beam radiation therapy to the head and neck completed ≥3 years before screening. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Absence of active Epstein‑Barr virus infection (negative anti‑VCA IgM); negative testing for active hepatitis C virus infection; negative testing for human immunodeficiency virus infection. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of active Epstein‑Burkitt virus infection, active hepatitis C virus infection, or HIV infection.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of recurrent head and neck cancer, parotid gland cancer, or disallowed second primary cancer; systemic autoimmune disease involving the salivary glands (e.g., Sjögren syndrome); current use of systemic immunosuppressive agents; active viral infections including EBV (positive anti‑VCA IgM), hepatitis C, or HIV; diagnosis of myasthenia gravis; personal or familial predisposition to acute/chronic angle‑closure glaucoma; known allergy or hypersensitivity to glycopyrrolate; current cigarette/tobacco/vaping use or cessation within the last 3 years; current excessive alcohol consumption or abuse within the last 3 years (men >14 drinks/week, women >7 drinks/week in North America; >14 units/week in the United Kingdom); poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (HbA1c >7%).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: any endometrial carcinoma subtype. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent disease with measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: at least one prior systemic chemotherapy regimen for endometrioid carcinoma **and** prior exposure to a programmed death‑1/programmed death‑ligand 1 checkpoint inhibitor (any setting). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: ARID1A loss‑of‑function mutation identified by a CLIA‑certified next‑generation sequencing assay. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Unresolved toxicities from prior anticancer therapy greater than Grade 1 (except medically manageable residuals).
• Concurrent receipt of any other investigational therapeutic agent.
• Presence of uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases; meninges carcinomatosa excluded.
• Documented severe hypersensitivity reaction (Grade ≥3) to avelumab, M1774, or similar biologics.
• Active, uncontrolled infectious processes (excluding well‑managed HIV/HBV/HCV as detailed in protocol).
• Ongoing systemic immunosuppressive therapy exceeding physiological glucocorticoid equivalents (>10 mg prednisolone daily) aside from approved peri‑procedure prophylaxis.
• Active autoimmune diseases requiring systemic immunosuppression (certain endocrine auto­immune conditions permissible if off immunosuppression).
• Previous solid‐organ or hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation.
• Significant gastrointestinal pathology (acute bowel obstruction, uncontrolled diarrhea, active inflammatory bowel disease).
• Psychiatric illness or social situation judged to compromise adherence (incl. recent suicidality).
• Major cardiovascular instability (uncontrolled hypertension, NYHA Class III–IV heart failure, uncontrolled arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, cerebrovascular accident/TIA within 180 days).
• Substance dependence (alcohol or illicit drugs).
• Other current malignancy unless disease free ≥5 years (exceptions: in‑situ breast/cervical carcinoma, basal/squamous skin carcinoma up to 5 years).
• Recent live or attenuated vaccinations within 28 days (inactive vaccines permitted).
• Concomitant use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 or 1A2 modulators, proton‑pump inhibitors, or other prohibited medications within 7 days.
• Consumption of herbal/natural “folk” remedies (e.g., St John’s wort, kava, ephedra, Ginkgo biloba, DHEA, yohimbine, saw palmetto, ginseng).
• Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures for women of childbearing potential.
• Baseline organ‑functional derangements outside protocol limits (ANC <1500/mm³, hemoglobin <9 g/dL, platelets <100 ×10⁹/L, bilirubin >1.5 × ULN [≤3 × ULN if Gilberts], transaminases >2.5 × ULN, creatinine clearance/GFR <60 mL/min).
• Prolonged corrected QT interval >470 ms persisting despite electrolytic optimization.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: female and male. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: any invasive breast cancer histologic subtype. Cancer burden allowed: clinical stage III inflammatory breast cancer (diagnosed within past 6 months). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic anti‑cancer therapy for the current diagnosis (chemotherapy, immunotherapy, targeted therapy); any prior radiotherapy for the current diagnosis; any prior definitive surgery for the current diagnosis. Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive (according to current ASCO/CAP guidelines) or HER2‑low (IHC 2⁺/ISH –, IHC 1⁺/ISH –, or IHC 1⁺/ISH untested); estrogen receptor and progesterone receptor status must be known. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of (non‑infectious) interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or unresolved/current ILD/pneumonitis, or inability to rule out ILD/pneumonitis on imaging.
Underlying pulmonary disorders (pulmonary embolism within 3 months, severe asthma, severe COPD, restrictive lung disease, pleural effusion) or autoimmune/connective‐tissue disease with pulmonary involvement, prior pneumonectomy.
Corrected QT interval >470 ms on screening ECG or any condition/drug increasing risk of QT prolongation or ventricular arrhythmias.
Cardiovascular events within 6 months: coronary artery bypass graft, angioplasty, vascular stent placement, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, NYHA class II or greater heart failure, stroke; left ventricular ejection fraction <50%.
Active or untreated central nervous system metastasis, leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.
Uncontrolled concurrent illness: active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, severe GI disease causing diarrhoea, psychiatric illness limiting compliance.
Chronic systemic glucocorticoid (>10 mg prednisolone/day) or other immunosuppressive therapy within the preceding 2 years (excluding physiological replacements).
Autoimmune or inflammatory disorders deemed active (except specified tolerated conditions such as vitiligo, alopecia, well‑controlled hypothyroidism, dietary‑managed celiac disease, chronic skin conditions not needing systemic therapy) without PI approval.
Recent (<3 months) venous thromboembolic event.
Active viral infections: hepatitis B surface antigen positive, hepatitis C PCR positive, active tuberculosis, HIV positive.
Use of live attenuated vaccines within 30 days prior to starting study treatment.
Concurrent strong cytochrome P450 enzyme modulators (potent CYP3A4, CYP2C9, CYP2D6 inhibitors/inducers) started within 2 weeks (St John’s wort within 3 weeks) of day 1 dosing.
Hydroxychloroquine taken less than 14 days before day 1 of trastuzumab deruxtecan.
Pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness to use adequate contraceptive measures as detailed in the protocol.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or lactation.
Known history of Wilson’s disease or other copper‑metabolism disorder.
Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%.
Creatinine clearance lower than 30 mL/min.
Aspartate aminotransferase or alanine aminotransferase greater than three times the upper limit of normal, or total bilirubin greater than twice the upper limit of normal (except up to fivefold increase in documented Gilbert’s syndrome).
Zubrod/ECOG performance status higher than 3.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional anticancer trial or receipt of prohibited investigational agents.
Unstable or life‑threatening comorbid illnesses deemed by investigator to compromise safety or data interpretation.

Other invasive malignancy within the preceding five years (except non‑melanoma skin cancer without current disease and remote treated breast cancer).  
Synchronous primary invasive ovarian or cervical cancer.  
Active or historic autoimmune disease or immune deficiency.  
History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on baseline imaging.  
Active tuberculosis.  
Severe infections.  
Significant cardiovascular disease.  
Administration of a live attenuated vaccine within four weeks before enrollment or anticipated need during study participation.  
Previous allogenic bone‑marrow transplantation or solid‑organ transplantation.  
Untreated central nervous system disease, uncontrolled seizures, or recent (< six months) cerebrovascular events (stroke, transient ischaemic attack, subarachnoid haemorrhage).

Prior treatment required: At least two prior lines of endocrine therapy in the recurrent/metastatic setting **and** receipt of a CDK4/6 inhibitor in combination with endocrine therapy in the recurrent/metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: Chemotherapy in the recurrent/metastatic setting **and** prior Aurora Kinase A–specific or pan‑Aurora‑Kinase agents (including alisertib) in any setting. Biomarkers required: HR‑positive and HER2‑negative tumor status (per local laboratory testing according to ASCO/CAP or ESMO guidelines). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: None

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: birth–21 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: high‑grade glioma. Histology allowed: anaplastic astrocytoma and glioblastoma and H3 K27‑mutant diffuse midline glioma. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed disease; disseminated disease accepted only if treated with chemotherapy alone (no craniospinal radiotherapy); primary spinal tumours accepted if treated with chemotherapy or focal radiotherapy; leptomeningeal‐only disease may be enrolled upon sponsor approval. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior anti‑cancer chemotherapy; any prior anti‑cancer agent including immunotherapy, monoclonal antibody, biological or targeted therapy; concurrent participation in another investigational drug study. Biomarkers required: NTRK fusion identified by FDA‑cleared/FDA‑approved method (FISH, PCR, or next‑generation sequencing) in a CLIA/CAP‑certified laboratory (testing may be centralized). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Current or recent receipt of another investigational drug. Previous exposure to any anti‑cancer agents (chemotherapy, immunotherapy, monoclonal antibodies, biologics, targeted therapies). Active, uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, or fungal infection. History of solid organ transplantation. Malabsorptive syndromes or conditions impairing oral drug absorption. Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers. Unable to comply with study safety monitoring procedures.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including, but not limited to, ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina pectoris, unstable cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness/social situations that would limit the safety of a biopsy and/or surgery.
Uncorrectable bleeding or coagulation disorder known to cause increased risk with surgical or biopsy procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: First‑line disease treated with a covalent Brutonʼs Tyrosine Kinase Inhibitor showing at least a partial response per iwCLL criteria and no evidence of transformation to aggressive non‑Hodgkin lymphoma (Richterʼs transformation). Prior treatment required: Ibrutinib **OR** Acalabrutinib **OR** Zanubrutinib taken continuously for ≥6 months at a stable dose. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior B‑cell lymphoma (Bcl‑2) inhibitor therapy **AND** any prior anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of cardiomyopathy.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease.
Active bleeding or history of bleeding diathesis.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anemia.
Uncontrolled immune thrombocytopenia.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Cutaneous melanoma. Histology allowed: Malignant melanoma. Cancer burden allowed: Completely resected, node‑positive (regional lymph node metastasis) disease with no residual locoregional or distant metastasis—including absence of brain metastasis—and classified as high risk for recurrence. Prior treatment required: Completion of surgical excision of the primary cutaneous melanoma together with removal of involved regional lymph node(s) within 13 weeks prior to randomization, achieving disease‑free status. Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of systemic anticancer therapy (e.g., cytotoxic chemotherapy, molecularly targeted agents, immune checkpoint inhibitors) other than interferon prescribed for a thick primary melanoma. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active autoimmune disease; immunodeficiency; receipt of systemic corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive therapy within seven days of starting protocol therapy; solid organ or allogeneic bone marrow transplantation; history of pneumonitis or prior interstitial lung disease; clinically significant heart failure; documented Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection; active hepatitis B or C infection; ongoing infectious process necessitating therapeutic intervention.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable disease as defined by at least one of the following: peripheral blood absolute B‑cell count >5,000 cells/μL, bone‑marrow infiltrates >30 %, palpable splenomegaly or lymph node masses >1.5 cm (meeting International Workshop on CLL criteria for treatment). Prior treatment required: Minimum two prior systemic therapies for CLL, one of which must have included a covalent Bruton’s Tyrosine Kinase inhibitor–containing regimen. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of prior venetoclax or phosphoinositide‑3‑kinase δ inhibitor treatment within the last 6 months (treated as a washout period and therefore not reflected in the space).
Receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days before starting study therapy.
Requirement for continuous high‑dose immunosuppressants or systemic glucocorticoids greater than 20 mg/day prednisone equivalents.
Uncontrolled autoimmune cytopenias (autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia) necessitating >20 mg/day prednisone.
Transformation of chronic lymphocytic leukemia to aggressive non‑Hodgkin lymphoma (Richter’s transformation) or pro‑lymphocytoid leukemia.
Residual toxicities from prior anticancer therapy exceeding Grade 1 (except alopecia or fatigue) or incomplete recovery from prior immunotherapy-related adverse events.
Concurrent receipt of any other investigational agent for chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
Allergic reaction to rog​inolisisb, venetoclax, rituximab, or structurally related compounds occurring within the past 6 months.
Presence of systemic autoimmune diseases.
Use of strong or prohibited moderate CYP3A enzyme modulators incompatible with venetoclax dosing.
Pregnancy or current breastfeeding; inadequate contraception adherence.
Active second primary malignant neoplasm expected to interfere with study assessments (exceptions limited to adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, cervix intraepithelial neoplasia, low‑risk prostate cancer, etc.).
Incapacity to ingest oral medication owing to gastrointestinal obstruction or dysphagia.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease, including baseline corrected QT interval > 470 ms.
Tuberculosis treatment completed less than two years ago.
Ongoing systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring IV antimicrobials (prophylactic agents permitted if stable).
Known HIV infection where antiretroviral therapy interferes with venetoclax metabolism.
Any serious, unmanaged medical illness or active infection judged by investigator to raise undue risk.
Documented alcohol or illicit substance dependence.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Biliary tract cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced, unresectable, or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Disease progression after prior systemic antitumour therapy or lack of satisfactory alternative therapeutic options. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: HER2 IHC 3+ or HER2 IHC 2+. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of somatic HER2 (ERBB2) DNA mutation without tumour HER2 protein expression.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease that required steroids, current interstitial lung disease, or suspicion of interstitial lung disease that cannot be ruled out by imaging at screening.
Lung‑specific intercurrent clinically significant severe illnesses.
Uncontrolled infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, antiviral agents, or antifungal medications.
Pleural effusion, ascites, or pericardial effusion that requires drainage, placement of a peritoneal shunt, or Cell‑free and Concentrated Ascites Reinfusion Therapy (CART).
Medical conditions that may interfere with the participant’s ability to comply with study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor. Histology allowed: any solid tumor histology. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced, unresectable, or metastatic disease with measurable lesions per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: ≥ 1 prior line of systemic chemotherapy for advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: KRAS G12D mutation identified in tumor tissue or circulating tumor DNA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Have known active central nervous system metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis.
Unresolved toxicities from prior therapy greater than CTCAE version 5.0 Grade 1.
Significant cardiovascular disease including unstable angina, acute coronary syndrome, history of myocardial infarction, or known reduced left ventricular ejection fraction.
Active uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection.
Known active hepatitis B virus infection or hepatitis C virus infection.
Other active malignancy unless in remission with life expectancy greater than 2 years.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: 1-3 prior chemotherapeutic regimens for metastatic breast cancer, including at least one regimen containing an antibody‑drug conjugate. Prior treatment excluded: progression on prior platinum chemotherapy in the metastatic setting; prior therapy with a WEE1 kinase inhibitor. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor negative and progesterone receptor negative (≤10% positivity by IHC) and HER2 negative (IHC 0 or 1+ or ISH ratio < 2.0); these will be assessed at screening. Biomarkers excluded: none.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Major surgical procedure less than 28 days before treatment start.
Current grade ≥2 peripheral neuropathy or other grade ≥2 toxicity (except alopecia or anorexia) attributable to prior therapy.
Allergic reaction to components of azenosertib, carboplatin, or pembrolizumab; severe hypersensitivity (grade ≥3) to pembrolizumab or its excipients.
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease requiring steroids, or current pneumonitis/ILD.
Untreated, symptomatic, or actively progressing brain metastases; recent neurosurgical resection or brain biopsy within 1 month preceding Day 1.
Presence of a percutaneous endoscopic gastro­stomy tube or reliance on total parenteral nutrition.
Use of prohibited concomitant medications, including strong or moderate CYP3A4/​CYP3A5 inhibitors or inducers, P‑glycoprotein modulators, or agents associated with marked QT‐interval prolongation.
Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illness such as active infection, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia, New York Heart Association class III or IV congestive heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (within 6 months), uncontrolled diabetes mellitus, recent gastritis or peptic ulcer disease (within 6 months), chronic liver disease, chronic kidney disease, or severe malnutrition.
Psychiatric disorder or psychosocial situation compromising adherence to study protocols.
Refractory nausea/vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal disease, or extensive bowel resections preventing adequate oral absorption of study medication.
Baseline corrected QTcF ≥470 ms, congenital long QT syndrome, torsades de pointes susceptibility, or concurrent use of medicines known to increase risk of QT prolongation.
Active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection requiring systemic therapy.
Autoimmune disease necessitating systemic immunosuppression within the past two years (aside from physiological hormonal replacement).
Immunodeficiency or systemic corticosteroid therapy greater than 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent (or equivalent immunosuppressants) within 7 days prior to first dose.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or plans to conceive or sire a child during the study period up to 120 days after the last dose.
Previous allogeneic solid-organ or bone-marrow/stem‑cell transplantation.
Receipt of any investigational medicinal product or device within 4 weeks before study commencement.
Administration of a live‑virus or live‑attenuated vaccine within 30 days before the first dose (killed/inactivated vaccines are permitted).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 0-21 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: low‑grade glioma. Histology allowed: low‑grade glioma. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or progressively growing low‑grade glioma requiring radiographic confirmation of progression. Prior treatment required: At least one line of prior systemic anticancer therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Activating BRAF alteration detected by CLIA‑certified assay. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of any additional activating molecular alteration apart from the requisite BRAF alteration; Diagnosis of neurofibromatosis type 1 confirmed by genetic testing or established clinical criteria.
general_exclusion_criteria: None reported.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 0-21 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor. Histology allowed: Various solid‑tumour histologies (any). Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic solid tumor with radiographic evidence of progression. Prior treatment required: At least one line of prior systemic anticancer therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Documented activating RAF fusion (known or predicted) determined by CLIA‑certified assay. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of any additional activating molecular alteration apart from the requisite RAF fusion; Diagnosis of neurofungi­morphosis type 1 confirmed by genetic testing or established clinical criteria.
general_exclusion_criteria: None reported.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non small cell lung carcinoma (all histological subtypes). Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: No prior chemotherapy for metastatic disease; may have commenced osimertinib within 3 weeks before enrollment (optional). Prior treatment excluded: Prior chemotherapy for metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: Activating epidermal growth factor receptor mutation identified in pretreatment tumor tissue, cytology, or pleural fluid sample **and** detectable EGFR‑mutant circulating free DNA in plasma at Cycle 2 Day 1. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Recent radiotherapy within 7 days prior to study treatment onset. Major surgery within 14 days prior to study treatment onset. Clinically significant interstitial lung disease or pulmonary fibrosis. Receipt of an investigational agent within five half‑lives or three months, whichever is longer. Current use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inducing agents, herbs, or foods without ability to discontinue. Unresolved adverse events greater than Grade 1 (except alopecia and Grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy due to prior platinum). Severe or uncontrolled systemic illnesses (e.g., uncontrolled hypertension, active hemorrhage). Active infection including hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus. Chronic hepatitis B carriers requiring antiviral prophylaxis considerations. Significant cardiovascular disorders: QTc > 470 ms, serious rhythm/conduction disturbances, congestive heart failure, electrolytes deficiencies affecting QT interval, congenital Long QT syndromes, familial Sudden Cardiac Death predisposition. History of interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced lung injury, or radiation pneumonitis needing steroids. Known hypersensitivity to osimertinib or structurally related compounds. Conditions impairing swallowing or intestinal absorption preventing oral administration. Investigator judgment deeming participant unlikely to comply with study protocols.

Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unretractable, recurrent disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Programmed death ligand‑1 combined positive score <1 (determined by PD‑L1 IHC 22C3 PharmDX). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe allergic reactions or Grade ≥ 2 intolerance to cetuximab or other anti‑EGFR monoclonal antibodies or murine-derived proteins.
Active secondary malignancies (hematologic malignancy or solid tumour other than those stipulated) unless definitively treated with no evidence of disease for ≥2 years (excludes indolent lesions such as basal/squamous skin carcinomas, stage I prostate cancer, cervical intra‑epithelial neoplasia, ductal carcinoma in situ of the breast). Note: concurrent chronic lymphocytic leukaemia permissible in the cutaneous SCC cohort if not actively treated.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection with CD4⁺ count < 250 cells/mm³, or lacking adequate antiretroviral control (viral load ≥ 400 copies/ml) despite ≥4 weeks of stable ART.
Chronic hepatitis B infection with active disease not suppressed by antivirals.
Untreated or inadequately controlled hepatitis C infection (detectable viral load exceeding quantitative threshold).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both male and female. Cancer type allowed: diffuse glioma. Histology allowed: diffuse astrocytoma or oligodendroglioma or glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: first radiographic relapse after prior standard therapy; measurable disease not required (post‑surgery residual disease optional). Prior treatment required: prior receipt of temozolomide‐containing chemotherapy (up to 12 total cycles) together with radiotherapy as per standard frontline management. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to procarbazine/lomustine/vincristine (PCV) regimens or any individual component thereof, prior carmustine wafer implantation, prior bevacizumab, prior PARP inhibitor therapy, current use of investigational anticancer agent. Biomarkers required: none. Biomarkers excluded: none.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial.
- Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
- History of clinically significant cardiovascular disease (unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction, uncontrolled heart failure, arrhythmias, QTc prolongation exceeding gender‑specific thresholds).
- Uncontrolled hypertension or hypotension.
- Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 1.5× ULN or AST/ALT > 3× ULN) or renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min).
- Ongoing serious infectious processes, including untreated bacterial, fungal, viral infections or HIV positivity.
- Clinically evident uncontrolled brain edema or mass effect causing neurologic deterioration.
- Recent major surgery (other than intended resections for glioma) within four weeks preceding registration.
- Concurrent use of strong cytochrome P450 3A modulators incompatible with study medication, including potent enzyme‑inducing antiepileptics.
- Requirement for continuous full‑dose anticoagulation without achievable therapeutic monitoring parameters.
- Presence of active gastrointestinal disorders compromising oral drug absorption (active ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease flare, short‑gut syndrome).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both male and female. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma, isocitrate dehydrogenase–wildtype. Histology allowed: glioblastoma, WHO grade 4 (microscopic verification of necrosis and/or microvascular proliferation) **or** diffuse astrocytic glioma, IDH‑wildtype with any of the following molecular features: TERT promoter mutation, epidermal growth factor receptor gene amplification, chromosome 7 gain coupled with chromosome 10 loss, or fulfillment of c‑IMPACT‑NOW 3 criteria. Cancer burden allowed: first radiographic relapse with at least one bi‑dimensional contrast‑enhancing lesion measuring ≥10 mm in minimum diameter on MRI; postoperative residual measurable disease required if surgical debulking performed. Prior treatment required: completion of standard Stupp protocol consisting of concurrent temozolomide with radiotherapy followed by up to 12 cycles of adjuvant temozolomide. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to PCV or its components, prior carmustine wafer implantation, prior bevacizumab, prior PARP inhibitor therapy, current use of investigational anticancer agent. Biomarkers required: IDH‑status verified by immunohistochemistry showing lack of mutant protein; if IHC negative and patient age < 55 years, additional DNA sequencing/PCR confirming absence of canonical IDH1/IDH2 mutations. Assessment of IDH status performed locally during screening. Biomarkers excluded: none.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 2-39 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: diffuse midline glioma (including diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma). Prior treatment required: Standard‐of‐care radiation therapy planned to start within 6 weeks of diagnosis; temozolomide permitted concurrently at standard pediatric dose; dexamethasone permissible if stable or tapering for ≥3 days; bevacizumab allowable up to five doses. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior cranial or spinal irradiation; prior re‑irradiation; thalamic or cerebellar tumor location. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of pneumonitis; congestive heart failure or reduced left ventricular shortening fraction (<27%); corrected QT interval ≥470 ms or familial long QT syndrome; significant hepatic dysfunction (total bilirubin >1.5 × upper limit of normal or alanine aminotransferase >3 × ULN); impaired renal function (creatinine clearance <70 ml/min/1.73 m²); peripheral neutropenia (absolute neutrophil count <750/mm³) or thrombocytopenia (platelets <75,000/mm³ transfusion‑independent); arterial oxyhemoglobin saturation ≤92% on ambient air; uncontrolled infection or sepsis; HIV infection, chronic hepatitis B or C; autoimmune disease requiring systemic cytotoxic/immunosuppressive therapy; receipt of any other investigational anticancer agent simultaneously; active secondary malignancy requiring treatment within past 3 years; pregnancy or lactation, or lack of reliable contraception; substance abuse or alcohol dependence; allergy to study drug components; performance status Karnofsky <50 (≥17 y) or Lansky <50 (≤16 y).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: malignant plasma cell neoplasm. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease with measurable paraprotein level (serum M‑protein ≥ 0.5 g/dL OR urine M‑protein ≥ 200 mg/24h OR involved serum free light chain ≥ 100 mg/L with abnormal κ/λ ratio). Prior treatment required: at least two prior lines of therapy INCLUDING a proteasome inhibitor **and** an immunomodulatory imide drug **and** an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody. Prior treatment excluded: receipt of B‑cell maturation antigen–targeted therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant drug or alcohol abuse within the preceding six months.
Clinically significant neurological, psychiatric, endocrine, metabolic, immunologic, cardiovascular, pulmonary, or hepatic diseases within the preceding six months judged to interfere with study participation.
Central nervous system involvement of multiple myeloma.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell lineage with CD19 positivity. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after ≥2 prior systemic therapy lines (or after 1 line when no standard‑of‑care exists) with bone marrow infiltration ≥5 % blasts; must not have isolated extramedullary disease relapse, testicular involvement, or active central nervous system disease. Prior treatment required: At least two prior antileukemic therapy lines. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CD19 positivity (local laboratory assessment). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active central nervous system involvement by B‑ALL (presence of blasts in cerebrospinal fluid).
Isolated extramedullary disease relapse.
Testicular leukemia.
History or presence of clinically relevant central nervous system pathology (e.g., epilepsy, seizures, strokes, serious head injury, dementias, movement disorders, psychiatric illness) or prior grade 4 neurotoxicity associated with cellular therapies.
History of other malignant neoplasm (except predefined exemptions).
Unresolved adverse events graded ≥2 attributable to prior therapies.
Recent graft‑versus‑host disease requiring immunosuppressive medication within three weeks before starting AZD0486.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Triple‑negative phenotype (Estrogen‑receptor ≤5% and Progesterone‑receptor ≤5% by IHC, HER2‑negative per ASCO/CAP). Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable locally advanced or metastatic disease (bone‑only disease permitted; previously treated brain metastases permitted). Prior treatment required: Nil systemic therapy for metastatic disease (no prior chemotherapy, biologic therapy, anti‑PD‑1/PD‑L1/PD‑L2 agents, sacitrazumab govitecan, nor irinotecan or topoisomerase I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugates). Prior treatment excluded: Chemotherapy administered in the metastatic setting; Biologic therapy administered in the metastatic setting; Anti‑PD‑1/PD‑L1/PD‑L2 agents; Irinotecan or topoisomerase I‑ADCs at any time; Sacituzumab govitecan. Significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing drug absorption.
Pregnancy or lactation; women of child‑bearing potential and men must practice approved contraception throughout the study and for the stipulated post‑treatment intervals.

Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illness—including chronic liver disease/cirrhosis, chronic kidney disease, pancreatic disease, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, asthma exacerbations, or psychiatric/substance‑use disorders interfering with participation.
Significant cardiac disease: recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, uncontrolled angina, ventricular tachyarrhythmias (unless benign PVCs), supraventricular/nodal arrhythmias necessitating device implantation or refractory to medication, substantial valvular pathology impairing function, symptomatic pericarditis, unstable coronary ischemia, QTc > 500 ms, congenital Long QT syndrome, or any other clinically meaningful electrophysiological abnormality.
Baseline hematologic, hepatic, or renal parameters outside specified limits (leukocytes <3000/μL, ANC <1500/μL, platelets <100 k/μL, absolute lymphocytes <500/μL, hemoglobin <9 g/dL, AST/ALT >2.5×ULN (or >5×ULN with Gilbert’s), bilirubin >1.5×ULN, creatinine >1.5×ULN or CrCl <50 ml/min, INR >1.5, PT/PTT elevation uncorrected).
Use of high‑dose systemic glucocorticoids (dex­ametasoné >2 mg/day equivalent for ≥3 continuous days within 2 weeks of initiation) or other systemic immunosuppressive agents (methotrexate, azathioprine, cyclophosphamide, etc.) within six months preceding enrolment.
Concurrent administration of enzyme‑inducing antiseizure medicines, potent CYP3A4 inducers/inhibitors, or contraindicated herbals/pharmaceuticals (warfarin derivatives, strong CYP3A modifiers, seville orange/grapefruit products) without appropriate discontinuation intervals.
Recent major surgery (<4 weeks) or incomplete recovery from prior operative toxicity.
Pregnancy or lactation; women of child‑bearing potential unwilling/unable to comply with mandated contraception; men unable/willing to practice prescribed contraception.
Previous receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 30 days before study entry (incl. measles, mumps, rubella, varicella‑zoster, yellow fever, rabies, BCG, Typhi); intranasal influenza vaccine disallowed.
Documented active non‑infectious pneumonitis.
Any history of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia, or evolving marrow dysplasia suggesting transformation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active central nervous system metastases.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness, including protocol-defined cardiac disease.
Mean corrected QT interval (QTcF) greater than 470 ms.
Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%.
Major surgery within two weeks prior to first dose of study intervention.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Gastrointestinal abnormalities that may impair oral intake of study drug.
Actively bleeding (except hemorrhoidal or gingival bleeding).
History of prior or concurrent malignancy that could confound study assessments.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (metastatic or unresectable). Histology allowed: Any solid‐tumour histology. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease **or** locally advanced/unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Any number of prior anticancer regimens permitted; for tumour types with an FDA‑approved poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor, patient must have received a PARP inhibitor prior to enrollment. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic mutation (somatic or germline) in any of the following DNA‑damage‑repair genes—as identified by a CLIA‑certified assay: BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, RAD51C, RAD51D, ATM, BARD1, BLM, BRIP1, CDK12, FANCA, FANCC, FANCD2, FANE​C, FANCF, FANCM, MRE11A, NBN, RAD50, RAD51B. Biomarkers excluded: UGT1A1 genotype showing homozygosity for *28, homozygosity for *6, **or** compound heterozygosity (*28 plus *6).
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients who received cytotoxic chemotherapy or radiation therapy within 2 weeks (6 weeks for nitrosoureas or mitomycin C) prior to study entry.; Residual toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia).; Concurrent participation in another investigational drug study.; Known hypersensitivity to novobiocin or chemically related compounds.; Current use of strong CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers.; Recent use (within 14 days) of medicines that are substrates of BCRP/ABC​​G2, OATP1B1/B3/2B1, or OAT1/OAT3.; Intake of herbal or dietary supplements with recognized hepatotoxic risk.; Use of medications associated with prolonged QT interval or torsades de pointe within 14 days of starting study drug (excluding granisetron).; Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including unstable cardiovascular disease, serious infections, untreated hypertension, or symptomatic congestive heart failure.; Active or inadequately managed liver disease (acute liver injury, decompensated cirrhosis).; Pregnancy or lactation.; Women of childbearing potential or males unwilling to employ appropriate contraception throughout the study and for four months thereafter.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 Years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic or locally advanced unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Continuous EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor (erlotinib, gefitinib, afatinib, or osimertinib) for ≥6 weeks with documented radiographic disease progression while on therapy. Prior treatment excluded: anti‑HER3 antibody; topoisomerase I inhibitor; antibody‑drug conjugate containing an exatecan derivative. Biomarkers required: EGFR activating mutation (G719X or exon 19 deletion or L858R or L861Q) and, if treated with erlotinib/g efinit ib/afatinib, absence of EGFR T790M mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis (including radiation‑induced).
Active or symptomatic central nervous system metastasis or spinal cord compression (unless adequately treated and asymptomatic).
Unstable cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction, NYHA Class II‑IV heart failure, uncontrolled angina, atrial fibrillation/flutter requiring medication, left ventricular ejection fraction <50%, significant ECG abnormalities, QTc prolongation exceeding protocol limits).
Severe uncontrolled hypertension (>180/>110 mm Hg).
Significant ocular disease (clinically significant corneal disease).
Concurrent systemic steroids >10 mg prednisolone daily or other immunosuppressive therapy.
Pregnancy or lactation, or inadequate contraception.
Known uncontrolled HIV infection.
Active hepatitis B or C infection not meeting predefined virologic suppression criteria.
Any other active malignant neoplasm within the preceding 3 years (exceptions: adequately treated basal/squamous skin carcinoma, superficial bladder tumour Ta/Tis/T1, in‑situ carcinomas)."

Pregnancy or breastfeeding; women of child‑bearing potential not employing adequate contraception or lacking a negative pregnancy test within seven days before entry. Histology allowed: CD20 positive lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma/Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Cancer burden allowed: Requires indication for treatment based on International Workshop on Waldenström’s Macroglobulinaemia criteria (evidence of symptomatic disease such as hyperviscosity, cytopenias, organ damage, bulky adenopathy, splenomegaly, constitutional symptoms, or serum IgM > 2 times upper limit of normal). Prior treatment required: At least one prior therapeutic line that included BOTH an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody (e.g., rituximab) AND a Bruton's Tyrosine Kinase inhibitor (e.g., ibrutinib, zanubrutinib), administered alone or together, and that was discontinued owing to intolerance or progressive disease. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CD20 positivity demonstrated by immunophenotype or immunohistochemistry on bone marrow specimen. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants whose disease has transformed to aggressive lymphoma.
Patients with symptomatic or untreated severe hyperviscosity syndrome or IgM > 4000 mg/dL who cannot receive/plasmapherese prior to dosing.
Receipt of any other investigational anticancer agent.
Unresolved adverse events attributable to prior anticancer therapy persisting worse than Grade 1 (except alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).
Active uncontrolled infection necessitating inpatient care or IV antibiotics within four weeks of starting treatment.
Significant cardiovascular disease (NYHA class III–IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction < 45 %, unstable angina, myocardial infarction < six months ago, serious arrhythmias).
Serious central nervous system disorders (uncontrolled seizures, recent stroke, psychosis, dementia, CNS vasculitis, encephalitis).
Requirement for supplemental oxygen at rest to keep saturation > 92%.
Use of chronic immunosuppressive medication for non‑study reasons within 28 days, including systemic glucocorticoids ≥20 mg prednisolone equivalent daily.
Known or presumed central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia (CNS lesions, leptomeningeal disease) or past CNS malignancies without complete remission.
Pregnancy, lactation, or inability/desire not to comply with stringent contraception measures throughout the study and for twelve months thereafter.
Current problematic alcohol or illicit drug misuse, significant psychiatric illness, or socially unstable circumstances limiting protocol adherence.
History of hypersensitivity reaction to substances structurally/compositionally similar to epcoritamab.
Administration of a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within four weeks preceding enrollment.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Autologous stem cell transplantation performed within 12 weeks before first dose, OR allogeneic stem cell transplantation performed within 1 year before first dose.
* Any unresolved adverse event graded ≥ Grade 2 attributable to prior anticancer therapy.
* Presence of non‑secretory multiple myeloma, active plasma cell leukemia (≥20 % peripheral WBCs or >2.0×10⁹/L circulating plasma cells), Waldenström’s macroglobulinemia, light‑chain amyloidosis, or polyneuropathy‑organomegaly‑endocrinopathy‑monoclonal protein‑skin changes (POEMS) syndrome.
* Major surgical procedure conducted within 4 weeks preceding first dose or scheduled during study participation.
* Acute infectious episode requiring antimicrobial therapy occurring within 14 days prior to first dose.
* Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus or hypertension recorded within 14 days prior to first dose.
* Peripheral neuropathy of Grade 3 or greater, OR Grade 2 with associated pain evaluated within 2 weeks prior to first dose.

Additional arm‑specific exclusions include prior allo‑stem cell or solid organ transplants, autologous stem cell transplant within 100 days, historic or ongoing CNS disorders (stroke, seizures, vasculitis, degenerative disease), unresolved serious infections, active or historical autoimmune conditions needing systemic immunosuppression, recent (>4 weeks) use of systemic immunomodulatory or suppressive agents, prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy within 100 days (primary refractoriness excluded), lingering grade ≥3 immune‑related toxicities, baseline oral glucocorticoid dosage exceeding 25 mg prednisolone daily within 4 weeks, and planned/live attenuated vaccination during study participation or within 5 months post‑therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than or equal to 18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive carcinoma of the breast. Cancer burden allowed: pathology stage pT1c through pT3 with node-negative disease (pN0, including isolates tumor cells deemed N0). Prior treatment required: definitive breast surgery (total mastectomy, skin‑saving mastectomy, nipple‑saving mastectomy, or lumpectomy) with margins free of invasive tumor and ductal carcinoma in situ; axillary staging by sentinel node biopsy, targeted axillary dissection, or axillary lymph node dissection. Prior treatment excluded: any chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or biologic therapy directed at the current breast cancer; endocrine therapy lasting longer than six weeks for the current breast cancer; continuation of hormonally based contraceptives at enrollment. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive (at least 1 percent nuclei) and/or progesterone receptor positive (at least 1 percent nuclei); human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative by ASCO/CAP criteria; Oncotype DX recurrence score 21 to 25 or recurrence score 16 to 20 together with high clinical risk (defined as low histologic grade larger than 3 cm, intermediate grade larger than 2 cm, or high grade larger than 1 cm). Biomarkers excluded: HER2‑positive tumor; MammaPrint high‑risk (MP2) result; absence of required Oncotype DX recurrence-score data.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Definitive clinical or radiologic evidence of metastatic disease.
Pathologic T4 tumors, including inflammatory breast cancer.
History of prior ipsilateral or contralateral invasive breast cancer.
Life expectancy shorter than ten years owing to comorbid conditions.
Absolute neutrophil count lower than 1,200 per cubic millimeter.
Platelet count lower than 100,000 per cubic millimeter.
Hemoglobin concentration lower than 10 grams per deciliter.
Total bilirubin exceeding upper limit of normal (or greater than 1.5 times ULN for benign hyperbilirubinemia syndromes).
Aspartate aminotransferase or alanine aminotransferase greater than three times institutional upper limit of normal.
Glomerular filtration rate less than 30 milliliters per minute per 1.73 square meters.
New York Heart Association functional classification poorer than class IIb.
Non‑epithelial breast malignancies such as sarcoma or lymphoma.
Any radiation therapy, chemotherapy, or biologic therapy administered for the current breast cancer prior to study entry (excluding brief endocrine exposure of fewer than six weeks).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding at the time of study entry.
Active chronic hepatitis B infection unless suppressed with undetectable viral load; active hepatitis C infection unless successfully treated or with undetectable viral load.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non‑squamous non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑squamous. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or radiation recall pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; untreated brain metastasis.
Significant cardiovascular disease (e.g., myocardial infarction ≤6 months, unstable angina, NYHA class II‑IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction <50%, prolonged QTc >470 ms).
Severe hepatic impairment or bilirubin elevation exceeding protocol limits.
Renal insufficiency meeting protocol-defined thresholds.
Active infection requiring systemic antibiotics, including unresolved COVID‑19 infection.
Immunodeficiency conditions such as uncontrolled HIV/AIDS or active hepatitis B/C.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent malignancy requiring active treatment.
Any condition compromising ability to receive study therapy safely or interfere with evaluation according to investigator judgment.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or lactation.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including active infection.
Symptomatic congestive heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (<6 months), unstable angina, clinically significant uncontrolled heart disease, left ventricular ejection fraction <50%.
Significant arterial blood pressure abnormalities (systolic >160 mm Hg or <90 mm Hg).
Cardiac rhythm disturbances (bradycardia HR<50 bpm, prolonged QTc (>470 ms), unrecoverable QRS/QTc measurement).
Severe electrolyte disorders predisposing to torsades de pointes (hypokalaemia, hypomagnesaemia).
History of angina, pericarditis, CABG, or other major cardiac events within six months.
Known HIV infection requiring antiretroviral therapy.
Active hepatitis B virus infection or chronic/hepatitis C virus infection.
Poor performance status exceeding Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group grade 1 (e.g., Karnofsky score <60%).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of superset (“superscan”) findings on nuclear medicine bone scans.
Any concomitant malignant neoplasm that is currently progressing or has required systemic therapy within the preceding 3 years, other than the studied castration‑resistant prostate cancer, and judged to affect survival or disease evaluation.
Previous treatment with platinum‑containing chemotherapy regimens.
Prior exposure to poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase (PARP) inhibitors such as olaparib or rucaparib.
Earlier therapy with alpha or beta emitting radiotherapeutic isotopes other than stipulated (actinium‑225, strontium‑89, samarium‑153, rhenium‑186, rhenium‑188).
Use of anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications that cannot be held when platelets drop below 50,000/mm³.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure NYHA class III-IV, or clinically significant arrhythmias.
Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 1.5× upper normal limit or AST/ALT > 2.5× ULN) or severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min).
Active central nervous system involvement with untreated or symptomatic brain metastases.
Untreated infection with human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C, or inability to achieve adequate control according to protocol specifications.
Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group grade 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Hepatocellular carcinoma. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Systemic therapy AND radiation to the liver that would exceed normal tissue tolerance AND selective internal radionuclide therapy (SIRT or yttrium‐89 hepatic arterial therapy). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis (incl. idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis); active or historical autoimmune disease (unless meeting protocol‑specified exemption criteria); active tuberculosis; significant cardiovascular disease (New York Heart Association class II–IV, recent myocardial infarction or stroke within three months); significant vascular disease (requiring repair of aortic aneurysm or recent peripheral arterial thrombosis); major surgical procedure, open biopsy or substantial trauma within four weeks (abdominal surgeries/interventions within sixty days); other invasive malignancy within five years (aside from designated low‑risk tumors); severe infection within four weeks preceding enrolment; therapeutic antibiotic use within two weeks before starting study treatment; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem‑cell or solid‑organ transplantation; clinically significant bleeding disorder or coagulation abnormality; recent appreciable haemoptysis; current therapeutic anticoagulation or thrombolytics (prophylactic dosing exempt); recent gastrointestinal fistula, perforation or intra‑abdominal abscess (≤ six months); recent/intolerable intestinal obstruction (≤ six months); moderate or severe ascites; untreated or high‑risk oesophageal/gastric varices; overt hepatic encephalopathy; concomitant HBV and HCV infection; known HIV infection; symptomatic, progressive or untreated central nervous system metastases; receipt of live‑attenuated vaccination within four weeks; severe allergic reaction to murine/chinese‑hamster‑ovarian derived therapeutics; hypersensitivity to any component of atezolizumab or bevacizumab formulations; uncontrolled tumor‑related pain; uncontrollable pleural, pericardial or ascitic fluid accumulation necessitating frequent drainage; uncontrolled hypercalcaemia; participation in another investigational drug study within twenty‑eight days; recent exposure to systemic immunostimulatory agents (interferon, IL‑2) or systemic immunosuppressive medications (high‑dose steroids, cyclosporine, azathioprine, methotrexate, thalidomide, anti‑TNF agents) within stipulated windows.

Prior treatment required: Definitive gross‑total resection (or scheduled simultaneous resection) of the primary osseous tumor together with systemic chemotherapy containing cisplatin‑doxorubicin or ifosfamide‑based regimen, either (a) current receipt or completion within 60 days for newly diagnosed disease **or** (b) completed prior to enrollment for patients experiencing first recurrence. Active infections requiring intravenous antibiotics.
Known untreated or progressive viral hepatitis B/C or HIV infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inadequate hematopoietic reserve (ANC <1500/mm³, platelets <100,000/mm³, hemoglobin <9 g/dL).
Concurrent malignant neoplasm requiring active therapy.
Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group grade 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Follicular lymphoma. Histology allowed: Grade 1‒Grade 3A, CD20 positive. Biomarkers required: Tumor CD20 positivity verified on pretreatment biopsy/sample (assessment performed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy greater than prednisone 10 mg/day (or equivalent).
Bulky cervical adenopathy threatening upper airway patency.
Stage I follicular lymphoma.
Candidates suitable for curative‑intent radiation therapy.
Major surgery or significant trauma within four weeks before study initiation, or incomplete recovery from such procedures.
Active chronic hepatitis B infection (HBV DNA PCR‑positive) or active hepatitis C infection (HCV RNA PCR‑positive).
Known HIV infection.
Active bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic, or other serious infection at enrollment (excluding superficial nail fungus) or significant infection within two weeks preceding first dose.
History of another malignancy (except non‑melanoma skin cancer or in‑situ cervical or breast carcinoma) unless disease‑free for ≥2 years.
Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within seven days of study entry or administration of any live vaccine during the study.
Severe/uncontrolled comorbidities compromising safety or protocol adherence.
Cardiac events occurring within six months: myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary or peripheral arterial bypass graft, congestive heart failure NYHA class III‑IV, left ventricular ejection fraction <45 %; clinically significant arrhythmias, long QT syndrome, torsades de pointe, or notable electrocardiographic abnormalities.
Recent stroke or transient ischemic attack.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during the study.
Previous solid‑organ or allogeneic stem cell transplantation.
Past or presumed haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.
Autoimmune disorders (e.g., myocarditis, pneumonitis, Myasthenia Gravis, polymyositis, autoimmune hepatitis, systemic lupus erythematosus, rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, antiphospholipid syndrome, Granulomatosis with polyangiitis, Sjögren’s syndrome, Guillain‑Barré syndrome, multiple sclerosis, vasculitis, glomerulonephritis) unless remotely historic and adequately controlled after sponsor investigation.
Documented severe hypersensitivity or anaphylaxis related to anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibodies or any component of epcoritamab.
Active central nervous system lymphoma.
Peripheral neuropathy worse than grade 2 (CTCAE).
Confirmed active SARS‑CoV‑2 infection or unresolved COVID‑19 symptoms/signs; inadequate virologic clearance according to protocol specifications.
Baseline corrected QT interval >470 milliseconds on screening ECG.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Prior treatment required: Completion of neoadjuvant cytotoxic chemotherapy prior to definitive surgery with pathologically clear margins (lumpectomy with subsequent whole‑breast irradiation or mastectomy) and axillary surgery as applicable; surgery and any required radiation must be finished within 4 months before enrolment. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior administration of sacituzumab govitecan, irinotecan, or any topoisomerase‑I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugate for early‑stage disease; any adjuvant chemotherapy (chemotherapy delivered after surgery); documented hypersensitivity to atezolizumab, sacituzumab govitecan, or their excipients. Biomarkers required: HER2 negative (determined by either in‑situ hybridisation non‑amplified [ratio < 2.0 or average HER2 copies < 4] **or** IHC score 0 or 1+); estrogen receptor negative (**< 10 %**) **and** progesterone receptor negative (**< 10 %**); circulating tumour DNA positively detected in a blood specimen obtained after completion of all locoregional therapy (central assay detecting any tumor‑specific mutation). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on imaging; congestive heart failure NYHA class III or IV, recent myocardial infarction (<6 months), unstable angina, serious uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia or other significant cardiac disease; active tuberculosis or other serious infectious diseases; uncontrolled type I or II diabetes mellitus; grade ≥ 2 uncontrolled hypercholesterolaemia, hypertriglyceridaemia or hypercalcaemia; severe liver disease such as cirrhosis; active bleeding diatheses; impaired gastrointestinal function affecting drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhoea grade > 2, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections); congenital long QT syndrome or baseline QTc > 480 ms; HIV positive, hepatitis B surface antigen positive, or hepatitis C viral RNA positive; pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability/unwillingness to use approved contraception (hormonal methods unacceptable); participation in another interventional oncology trial concurrently; unresolved (>Grade 1) treatment‑related toxicities (except alopecia and peripheral neuropathy); major surgery (other than diagnostic procedures) within 4 weeks preceding study treatment; recent systemic antimicrobial therapy within 2 weeks (prophylaxis exempted); recent systemic immunosuppressive medications within 2 weeks (low‑dose steroids for allergies, COPD, orthostatic hypotension, or mineralocorticoid replacement exempted); prior invasive breast cancer in either breast; other prior malignant solid tumours unless disease‑free ≥5 years (thyroid cancer exemption noted).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: male and female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: any breast cancer histology. Active herpetic infection or prior complicated HSV‑1 infection (herpetic keratitis or encephalitis).
Systemic infection requiring IV antibiotics or antivirals, or other serious infection within 60 days before dosing.
Requirement for intermittent or chronic systemic anti‑viral agents with anti‑herpetic activity (unless for graft preservation).
Need for CTLA‑4–Ig medications.
Significant autoimmune disease treated with systemic immunosuppression beyond routine graft prophylaxis within past 5 years.
Active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or HIV infection.
Transplant‑related viral infections (BK polyomavirus, Epstein‑Barr virus, Cytomegalovirus) within 3 months; detectable HBV or HCV viral loads within 3 months.
Condition necessitating escalation of regular immunosuppressive regimen within 60 days of study drug initiation.
Known active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑small cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic or unresectable locally advanced disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any antibody‑drug conjugate targeting epidermal growth factor receptor or HER3 or containing a topoisomerase Ⅰ payload; more than two prior systemic chemotherapy regimens; prior epidermal growth factor receptor exon 20 insertion mutation as primary EGFR alteration; cumulative anthracycline exposure greater than 360 mg/m²; prior autologous or allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. general_exclusion_criteria: History of symptomatic congestive heart failure grade ≥2, NYHA class ≥2, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or other severe heart disease.; Prolonged corrected QT interval >470 ms, complete left bundle branch block, or grade 3 atrioventricular block.; Active autoimmune or inflammatory disorders requiring systemic therapy (e.g., systemic lupus erythematosus, moderate-to-severe psoriasis, rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, Hashimoto’s thyroiditis) except well‑controlled endocrine disorders.; Additional malignant tumors diagnosed within five years preceding enrollment, except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin carcinoma or carcinoma in situ.; Poorly controlled hypertension (SBP >150 mmHg or DBP >100 mmHg despite two agents.).; Interstitial lung disease or prior pneumonitis of grade ≥3.; Recent (<6 months) unresolved venous or arterial thromboembolic events requiring therapeutic anticoagulation.; Untreated or actively progressing central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; stable treated brain metastases permitted only if off steroids >20 mg prednisolone equivalents daily and neurologically stable for ≥4 weeks.; Allergy to recombinant humanised or mouse‑chimaeric antibodies or any excipient of the investigational agent.; Positive testing for HIV, active hepatitis B or C infection, or active tuberculosis.; Ongoing acute infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.; Pregnancy or lactation.; Any condition judged by the investigators to render participation unsafe.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: early-stage, surgically resected, invasive breast cancer without distant metastasis or inflammatory breast cancer, classified as high risk of recurrence according to clinicopathologic features. Prior treatment required: 24 to 60 months of adjuvant endocrine therapy for the current breast cancer (continuous with no gap greater than 6 months and completed ≤6 months before screening); may have additionally received neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy and/or a cyclin-dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor or poly ADP ribose polymerase inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: prior breast cancer chemoprevention with tamoxifen, aromatase inhibitor, or raloxifene. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (assessment performed at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of metastatic disease (including contralateral axillary lymph node involvement) or inflammatory breast cancer.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or planned conception/fatherhood during the study period (from screening through 180 days after last dose).
Previous diagnosis of another malignant solid tumour or hematologic malignancy (aside from appropriately treated ipsilateral ductal carcinoma in situ ≥5 years ago).
Serious pre‑existent medical conditions judged by the investigator to interfere with safe participation.
Performance status worse than ECOG 1.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: plasma cell neoplasm consistent with multiple myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed and refractory disease with documented progressive disease on or within 60 days after most recent therapy, not primary refractory disease. Prior treatment required: minimum one prior therapeutic line INCLUDING ≥2 cycles of lenalidomide AND ≥2 cycles of a proteasome inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody receipt within the past six months OR any other multiple myeloma directed monoclonal antibody within the past thirty days OR any anti‑BCMA therapy at any time. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Presence of current moderate or worse corneal epithelial disease.
- Requirement to wear contact lenses during the study.
- Receipt of any investigational antimyeloma agent concurrently.
- Concurrent high‑dose corticosteroid therapy exceeding prednisolone 10 mg/day equivalence.
- Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of reliable contraception according to protocol specifications.
- Prior history of malignancy other than multiple myeloma, unless meeting predefined remission duration exemptions.
- Known human immunodeficiency virus infection.
- Positive hepatitis B surface antigen (active chronic Hepatitis B infection) or untreated active hepatitis C infection.
- Persistent peripheral neuropathy graded ≥2 despite optimal management.
- Severe allergic reaction or hypersensitivity to isatuximab, belantamab mafodotin, pomalidomide, or dexamethasone.
- Allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation less than twelve months ago combined with ongoing immunosuppression or active graft‑versus‑host disease.
- Autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplantation less than six months ago.
- Serious cardiovascular illnesses such as New York Heart Association class III–IV heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant uncontrollable arrhythmias, myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident within the preceding six months, or uncontrolled arterial hypertension.
- Metabolic disorder of diabetes mellitus associated with two or more ketotic episodes in the past year.
- Advanced chronic obstructive pulmonary disease necessitating more than two inpatient exacerbations over the past year.
- Unstable hepatic or biliary pathology.
- Ongoing acute bacterial, viral, or fungal infection.
- Other substantial medical, psychiatric, or psychosocial conditions deemed prohibitive by investigators.
- Major surgical procedures performed within four weeks prior to enrollment.
- Administration of live or attenuated vaccinations within thirty days before dosing.
- Residual toxicity from previous anticancer therapy greater than grade 1 (excluding alopecia and stable peripheral neuropathy).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 55 years and older. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumour (any anatomical origin except prostate, breast, testicular or adrenal gland). Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: Active disease (patient is presently receiving or has received chemotherapy and/or radiation therapy) or no‑evidence‑of‑disease state documented ≤60 months since completion of most recent chemotherapy and/or radiation therapy. Prior treatment required: Chemotherapy and/or radiation therapy (received or currently receiving). Prior treatment excluded: Use of anabolic agents (testosterone, dehydroepiandrosterone, growth hormone). Biomarkers required: Total testosterone <348 ng/dL or free testosterone <70 pg/mL (measured by mass spectroscopy). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of prostate, breast, testicular or adrenal cancer.
Serum haemotocrit >48%.
Serum creatinine >2.5 mg/dL.
Prostate‑specific antigen >4 ng/mL or abnormal digital rectal examination findings.
Severe untreated obstructive sleep apnoea.
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure.
Myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndrome, cardiac revascularisation, cerebrovascular accident or arterial/thrombotic event occurring within six months.
Known inherited thrombophilia (e.g., factor V Leiden).
Previous stroke leaving persistent neurological deficit.
Incapacity to give informed consent (Mini–Mental State Examination score <24).
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (HbA₁c >10%).  
Body-mass index greater than 40 kg/m².  
Bipolar disorder or schizophrenia.
