general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis; symptomatic congestive heart failure; unstable angina or significant cardiac arrhythmia; uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active tuberculosis infection; active or chronic viral hepatitis B or C infection; human immunodeficiency virus infection; ongoing systemic steroid or immunosuppressive therapy for autoimmune disease (except permitted mild/moderately treated conditions); active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy; known hypersensitivity to epacadostat, pembrolizumab, fluorouracil, oxaliplatin, or related compounds; current anticoagulation with warfarin; personal history of serotonin syndrome; abnormal electrocardiogram judged clinically significant; gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption; receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 30 days before dosing; pregnancy or lactation; inability to comply with contraception requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: plasma cell malignancy. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease with documented progression after most recent therapy and measurable disease. Prior treatment required: receipt of prior lines of therapy that together include an immunomodulatory drug, a proteasome inhibitor, an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody, and a T‑cell redirecting therapy (chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell product or T‑cell‐engaging bispecific antibody) unless the patient is not a candidate for such therapy; disease must be refractory to or have relapsed after these agents. Prior treatment excluded: prior therapy with CC‑92480; prior therapy with tazemetostat; prior therapy with BMS‑986158; prior therapy with trametinib; any prior allogeneic stem‑cell transplant; autologous stem‑cell transplant performed ≤12 weeks before enrolment. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active or historical central nervous system involvement of multiple myeloma.
Presence of plasma cell leukemia.
Diagnosis of Waldenström macroglobulinemia.
Polyneuropathy, organomegaly, endocrinopathy, M‑protein, and skin changes (POEMS) syndrome.
Clinically significant light‑chain amyloidosis.
Impaired cardiac function or clinically significant cardiovascular disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: <25 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Central nervous system low‑grade glioma. Histology allowed: Glioma. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior or ongoing non‑surgical anticancer therapy (chemotherapy, oral/IV targeted therapy, radiation). Biomarkers required: activatin​g RAF alteration. Biomarkers excluded: IDH1 mutation or IDH2 mutation, Histone H3 mutation, Neurofibromatosis type 1 loss‑of‑function alteration, Neurofibromatosis type 2 loss‑of‑function alteration.
general_exclusion_criteria: None

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: <25 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Central nervous system low‑grade glioma. Histology allowed: Glioneuronal tumor. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: Lymphoplasmatic lymphoma (Waldenström macroglobulinemia). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory symptomatic disease fulfilling consensus criteria (constitutional symptoms, cytopenias, bulky nodal or splenic disease, hyperviscosity, peripheral neuropathy, amyloid, renal insufficiency, cryoglobulinemia, serum IgM ≥ 2× ULN). Prior treatment required: At least two prior lines of therapy for Waldenström macroglobulinemia **and** (BTK inhibitor exposure **or** ineligibility for BTK inhibitor therapy). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Positive hepatitis B surface antigen; hepatitis B core antibody–positive with confirmatory HBV DNA positivity; hepatitis C antibody–positive with detectible HCV RNA; uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Uncontrolled HIV infection.
Active hepatitis B infection (HBsAg+) or hepatitis C infection (HCV RNA+).
Chronic liver disease classified as Child‑Pugh B or C.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intent to conceive during study or up to 3 months post‑treatment.
Central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia.
Active alcohol or substance misuse.
Concurrent use of moderate or strong CYP3A inhibitors or inducers within appropriate window.
Participation in another interventional oncology trial concurrently.
History of another malignant neoplasm except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, cervical intra‑epithelial neoplasia, superficial bladder cancer, localized prostate cancer, or other cancer in sustained complete remission.
Clinically significant acute illnesses or infections requiring antibiotic therapy judged to impact safety or pharmacokinetics.
Incapacity to ingest oral tablets.
Significant cardiovascular disease: unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤ 45 % within past year, NYHA class III‑IV heart failure, symptomatic arrhythmias, or QTc interval > 480 msec.
Ongoing active infection.
Active hemorrhage necessitating transfusion or invasive management (anticoagulants permitted).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: primary central nervous system lymphoma. Histology allowed: any histology diagnostic of primary central nervous system lymphoma (commonly diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease after prior CNS‑directed therapy (including primary refractory disease, ocular recurrence) with documented progression on magnetic resonance imaging or cerebrospinal fluid cytology. Prior treatment required: must have received prior CNS‑directed therapy (chemotherapy ± rituximab ± radiation). Prior treatment excluded: prior anti‑programmed death‑1, anti‑programmed death‑ligand 1, or anti‑programmed death‑ ligand 2 therapy; prior therapy targeting other stimulatory or inhibitory T‑cell receptors such as CTLA‑4, OX‑40, or CD137; prior progression on ibrutinib or any other Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients who cannot undergo MRI brain.
History of grade > 2 intracranial hemorrhage.
Concurrent use of warfarin or other vitamin K antagonist within 7 days of start.
Recent (<3 months) arterial thromboembolic event (stroke, MI).
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy or steroids >10 mg prednisone equivalents, or treated autoimmunity within the past 2 years.
Requirement for high‑dose systemic corticosteroids (dexamethasone >4 mg/day or equivalent for >3 consecutive days within 2 weeks of registration).
Unstable or unrecovered toxicities from prior therapy greater than grade 1 (excluding peripheral neuropathy ≤grade 2).
Major surgery within 2 weeks preceding treatment or planned within 2 weeks thereafter.
Radiation‑related toxicities not fully resolved; recent cranial irradiation within 2 weeks (unless appropriate washout applied).
Receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days before first dose.
Presence of another actively progressing malignant neoplasm or requiring treatment within the past 3 years (exceptions noted for certain cured skin carcinomas and in‑situ lesions).
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or ongoing pneumonitis.
Active systemic infection graded >2 or IV antibiotics administered ≤14 days before first dose.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease (uncontrolled/symptomatic arrhythmia, NYHA class III/IV heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within 6 months).
Uncontrolled hypertension despite optimized therapy.
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (HbA1c >8%) or steroid‑induced diabetes meeting same threshold.
Non‑healing wounds, ulcers, or fractures.
Known congenital or acquired bleeding diathesis (von Willebrand disease, hemophilia).
Inability to ingest oral capsule formulation or conditions causing marked GI absorption problems.
Concomitant potent/moderate CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers not stopped appropriately.
Use of enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants without substitution.
Positive serology/history for HIV/AIDS, active hepatitis B (HBsAg+) or hepatitis C (detectable HCV RNA), or active tuberculosis.
Previous allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Psychiatric illness or substance misuse impairing compliance.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant/paternal conception during study period and until 120 days after last dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 28 days to <18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: NA. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Diagnosis of high‑risk hematopoietic stem cell transplant associated thrombotic microangiopathy occurring after an allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant, fulfilling any one of the prespecified high‑risk laboratory or clinical criteria. Prior treatment required: receipt of an allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Prior treatment excluded: current or historical use of eculizumab or ravulizumab or defibrotide for any indication. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonia/pneumonitis. Uncontrolled cardiac insufficiency or congestive heart failure. Significant renal impairment (e.g., estimated glomerular filtration rate markedly reduced). Severe hepatic dysfunction (alanine transaminase or aspartate transaminase greater than five times the institutional upper limits of normal). Active untreated central nervous system involvement or uncontrolled brain metastasis. Current or chronic infectious diseases including unresolved bacterial, fungal, or viral infections needing ongoing therapeutic intervention. Positive serology for Human Immunodeficiency Virus or Hepatitis B/C viruses. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Recent participation in another interventional research protocol (within four weeks). Known allergy or hypersensitivity to components of the investigative medicinal product. Employment relationship or close familial connection to sponsor, CRO, investigators, or study personnel. Any medical condition judged by the investigator to place the participant at undue risk.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: chronic myelomonocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: CMML‑1 or CMML‑2. Cancer burden allowed: refractory or resistant disease defined as lack of response to ≥ four cycles of hypomethylating agents (or hydroxyurea for myeloproliferative‐type CMML) or discontinuation of such therapy owing to toxicity or documented disease progression. Prior treatment required: exposure to hypomethylating agents for at least four cycles **or** hydroxyurea (as appropriate for disease subtype). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Eligible for immediate allogeneic stem cell transplant; presence of myelodysplastic syndrome/myeloproliferative neoplasm overlap syndromes other than chronic myelomonocytic leukemia; acute promyelocytic leukemia or isolated extramedullary disease; prior bone marrow transplant within six months or receipt of immunosuppressive therapy within three months post‑transplant; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (excluding low‑dose corticosteroids ≤ 10 mg prednisone equivalents/day); current active use of nicotine products (tobacco, nicotine patch, vape); clinically significant cardiovascular disease—including NYHA class III–IV heart failure, uncontrolled/chest pain, myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident within six months, uncontrolled hypertension, or untreated arrhythmias; baseline corrected QT interval > 470 ms (Fridericia correction); concomitant active malignancy requiring therapy (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, organ‑confined prostate cancer without progression); active uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection; confirmed HIV infection; active or chronic hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection; any other severe or uncontrolled systemic disease, major psychiatric disorder, or condition compromising safety, informed consent, or protocol adherence.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: monocytic or monocytic‑predominant AML (defined by ≥ 50 % monocytes/monoblastic cells and positivity for at least two monocytic surface antigens [CD4, CD11c, CD14, CD36, CD64]; peripheral blood white‑blood‑cell count \< 30,000/μL and \< 20 % circulating blasts). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease after prior therapy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Eligible for immediate allogeneic stem cell transplant; presence of myelodysplastic syndrome/myeloproliferative neoplasm overlap syndromes other than chronic myelomonocytic leukemia; acute promyelocytic leukemia or isolated extramedullary disease; prior bone marrow transplant within six months or receipt of immunosuppressive therapy within three months post‑transplant; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (excluding low‑dose corticosteroids ≤ 10 mg prednisone equivalents/day); current active use of nicotine products (tobacco, nicotine patch, vape); clinically significant cardiovascular disease—including NYHA class III–IV heart failure, uncontrolled/chest pain, myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident within six months, uncontrolled hypertension, or untreated arrhythmias; baseline corrected QT interval > 470 ms (Fridericia correction); concomitant active malignancy requiring therapy (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, organ‑confined prostate cancer without progression); active uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection; confirmed HIV infection; active or chronic hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection; any other severe or uncontrolled systemic disease, major psychiatric disorder, or condition compromising safety, informed consent, or protocol adherence.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Hormone receptor‑positive, human epidermal growth factor receptor 2‑negative invasive carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Eligibility for fulvestrant and prior tolerance of at least one cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib or ribociclib or abemaciclib). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria: History of another primary malignancy except when cured with no evidence of disease for ≥2 years and low risk of recurrence. Recent radiotherapy (within 2 weeks) or major surgery/significant trauma (within 4 weeks). Persistent adverse effects greater than CTCAE grade 1 from prior anti‑cancer therapy (excluding alopecia). Active or inadequately controlled spinal cord compression, brain metastases, or leptomeningebral metastases unless fully treated and steroid‑free for ≥4 weeks. Significant cardiac abnormalities including prolonged QTc intervals (specific thresholds per concurrent CDK4/6 inhibitor), clinically important rhythm/conduction disturbances, recent (<6 months) severe cardiovascular interventions, NYHA class II or worse heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension or hypotension, reduced left ventricular ejection fraction <50%. Clinically significant disorders of glucose metabolism (type I or type II diabetes requiring insulin, HbA1c ≥8.0%). Prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Hormone receptor‑positive, human epidermal growth factor receptor 2‑negative invasive carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Documented prior endocrine therapy (tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitor or oral selective estrogen receptor degrader) given as single agent or in combination in the (neo)adjuvant setting with radiographic evidence of recurrence or progression while on therapy or within 12 months after completion; eligibility for fulvestrant and ability to receive at least one cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib or ribociclib). Prior treatment excluded: Prior treatment with AKT inhibitor or phosphoinositide‑3‑kinase inhibitor or mammalian target of rapamycin inhibitor; prior cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor use in the metastatic setting (use in adjuvant setting permissible if ≥12 months drug‑free); more than one line of chemotherapy for metastatic disease; any endocrine‑based therapy for inoperable locally advanced or metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: Central circulating tumor DNA assay for PIK3CA mutation, AKT1 mutation, or PTEN alteration performed on baseline blood sample. Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria: History of another primary malignancy except when cured with no evidence of disease for ≥2 years and low risk of recurrence. Recent radiotherapy (within 2 weeks) or major surgery/significant trauma (within 4 weeks). Persistent adverse effects greater than CTCAE grade 1 from prior anti‑cancer therapy (excluding alopecia). Active or inadequately controlled spinal cord compression, brain metastases, or leptomeningebral metastases unless fully treated and steroid‑free for ≥4 weeks. Significant cardiac abnormalities including prolonged QTc intervals (specific thresholds per concurrent CDK4/6 inhibitor), clinically important rhythm/conduction disturbances, recent (<6 months) severe cardiovascular interventions, NYHA class II or worse heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension or hypotension, reduced left ventricular ejection fraction <50%. Clinically significant disorders of glucose metabolism (type I or type II diabetes requiring insulin, HbA1c ≥8.0%). Prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 to 21 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: hepatoblastoma. Histology allowed: hepatoblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease with measurable lesion(s) per protocol and ≤50 % liver involvement. Prior treatment required: prior standard‑of‑care therapy resulting in relapse or refractory status. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: alpha‑fetoprotein >100 ng/mL and HLA‑A2 positive. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Recurrent disease amenable to complete surgical resection (e.g., isolated pulmonary relapse suitable for metastasectomy). Significant pre‑existing medical conditions including heart failure, uncontrolled non‑cancer pulmonary disease, or psychiatric/social situations limiting compliance. Active, uncontrolled systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection (subjects with HIV, hepatitis B or C acceptable if infection is treated and viral loads are controlled). Presence of any additional active malignant disease besides the target liver tumour. Pregnancy or lactation. Use of concomitant investigational agents, biologics, chemicals, or radiotherapies while enrolled. Known contraindication to conditioning chemotherapy agents fludarabine and cyclophosphamide. Active autoimmune disease necessitating systemic immunosuppressive therapy. Obstructive compromise of the main portal vein, hepatic veins, or inferior vena cava judged unsuitable for enrolment. History of solid‑organ transplantation. Infiltration of greater than 50 % of total liver volume by tumour.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 to 21 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: hepatocellular neoplasm—not otherwise specified. Histology allowed: hepatocellular neoplasm‑not otherwise specified. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease with measurable lesion(s) per protocol and ≤50 % liver involvement. Prior treatment required: prior standard‑of‑care therapy resulting in relapse or refractory status. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: alpha‑fetoprotein >100 ng/mL and HLA‑A2 positive. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease meeting International Workshop on Chronic Lymphocytic Leukaemia 2018 criteria for active disease. Prior treatment required: at least two prior systemic therapy regimens for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Allogeneic stem-cell transplantation within 6 months of trial entry.
Monoclonal antibody for antitumor therapy administered within 4 weeks of trial entry.
Side effects due to prior therapy not resolved to grade ≤ 1.
Oral targeted inhibitors ( Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors, B‑cell lymphoma 2 inhibitors, phosphoinositide 3‑kinase inhibitors ) taken within five half‑lives of dosing schedule.
Active viral, bacterial or systemic fungal infection requiring treatment.
Human immunodeficiency virus positivity.
Active known central nervous system lymphoma.
Pregnant or lactating individuals.
History of another malignancy diagnosed less than 2 years before enrollment, except adequately treated localized skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or prostate cancer managed with watchful waiting.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses such as New York Heart Association class III–IV congestive heart failure, myocardial infarction within past 6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction < 40 %, life‑ threatening cardiac arrhythmias, unstable angina, symptomatic brain metastases, or serious psychiatric disorders impairing protocol adherence.
Known hypersensitivity to any excipient in the investigational product.
Documented development of human anti‑drug antibodies.
Active autoimmune disease, immune‑mediated peripheral neuropathy, or other central nervous system autoimmune conditions.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: small lymphocytic lymphoma. Histology allowed: small lymphocytic lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease meeting International Workshop on Chronic Lymphocytic Leukaemia 2018 criteria for active disease. Prior treatment required: at least two prior systemic therapy regimens for small lymphocytic lymphoma. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Documented development of human anti‑drug antibodies.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 3–29 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Primary central nervous system non‑germinomatous germ cell tumor involving the suprasellar region, pineal region, or both (bifocal). Histology allowed: Endodermal sinus (yolk sac) tumor and/or embryonal carcinoma and/or choriocarcinoma and/or malignant/immature teratoma and/or mixed germ cell tumor containing any of these malignant nongerminomatous elements. Cancer burden allowed: Localized disease (no metastatic spread demonstrated by brain magnetic resonance imaging, spine magnetic resonance imaging, and negative cerebrospinal fluid cytology). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Systemic antitumor therapy (any chemotherapy, targeted agent, immunotherapy, hormone therapy, or radiotherapy) administered before enrollment, except for surgical resection/biopsy and corticosteroids. Biomarkers required: Serum or cerebrospinal fluid alpha‑fetoprotein ≥10 ng/mL **or** beta‑human chorionic gonadotropin ≥100 mIU/mL, verified by rapid centralized tumor marker review. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of significant cardiovascular disease (heart failure, recent myocardial infarction).
Uncontrolled hypertension or arrhythmia requiring medication adjustment.
Severe chronic respiratory illness (requiring home oxygen or mechanical ventilation).
Active infections including untreated viral hepatitis B/C, HIV/AIDS, or tuberculosis.
Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, ALT >135 U/L despite adjusted threshold) or renal insufficiency (glomerular filtration rate <70 ml/min/1.73 m²).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inability or unwillingness to use effective contraception throughout the study.
Known hypersensitivity to any investigational drug components.
Concurrent malignancy requiring active treatment.
Any condition compromising compliance with study procedures (severe psychiatric disorder, substance abuse, or inability to comprehend study instructions).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Glioblastoma, gliosarcoma. Histology allowed: Glioblastoma, gliosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: Intracranial disease after maximal safe surgical resection (tumor may be measurable or non‑measureable). Prior treatment required: Maximally safe surgical resection performed prior to enrolment. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic chemotherapy, any prior cranial radiotherapy, receipt of other investigational anticancer agents. Biomarkers required: IDH1 R132H immunohistochemical negativity **AND** O⁶‑methylguanine‑DNA methyltransferase (MGMT) promoter unmethylated by standard‐care assay (**plus** availability of whole‑exome / whole‑genome sequencing or copy‑number analysis for exploratory biomarker stratification). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of lower‑grade glioma whose subsequent pathology showed glioblastoma. Planned major surgery. Use of Optune™ device. Presence of a second malignant neoplasm unless disease‑free ≥ 2 years or meets predefined low‑risk exceptions (CIS cervix, superficial bladder CA, basal/squamous skin CA). Significant intra‑ or peri‑tumoral haemorrhage judged notable by clinician. Clinically significant cardiovascular disease (NYHA class ≥ II heart failure, recent MI ≤ 12 months, uncontrolled arrhythmias, prolonged QT/QTc thresholds, severe hypertension ≥ 160/95 mmHg, structural conduction blocks). Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (fasting glucose ≥ 200 mg/dL or HbA₁c ≥ 8%). Active serious intercurrent illnesses—including untreated infection, chronic viral hepatitis B/C, chronic liver disease, chronic kidney disease, pancreatitis, chronic pulmonary disease, or psychiatric conditions limiting adherence. Pregnancy or lactation. Gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (severe malabsorptive syndromes, unrepaired extensive small‑bowel resections, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhea Grade ≥ 2). Allergies to study‑drug related substances. Concurrent use of enzyme‑inducing antiseizure medicines, strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers, or vitamin/herbal products interacting with CYP3A4. Current use of warfarin or other coumarin anticoagulants.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Merkel cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: Neuroendocrine Merkel cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced unresectable or metastatic disease with measurable lesions according to RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: Documented receipt of an anti‑Programmed Death‑1 (PD‑1) or anti‑Programmed Death Ligand‑1 (PD‑L1) antibody (alone or in combination with other agents, e.g., ipilimumab) AND subsequent progressive disease occurring during therapy or within 120 days after the last dose of the anti‑PD/(L)1 agent. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Histology allowed: invasive carcinoma (ductal, lobular, or other invasive histologic subtype). Cancer burden allowed: de novo metastatic disease (stage IV at initial diagnosis), including patients with treated or stable brain metastases meeting stipulated stability criteria. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy for invasive breast cancer other than trastuzumab + pertuzumab + taxane administered ≤6 weeks before study start; prior investigational anticancer agents; prior endocrine therapy for invasive disease. Biomarkers required: HER2 overexpression defined as Immunohistochemistry score 3+ on both primary breast tumour and metastatic sites according to ASCO/CAP guidelines, verified in a CLIA-certified or ISO-accredited laboratory. Biomarkers excluded: HER2 IHC 1+ or 2+ without confirmatory FISH amplification.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Prior history of invasive breast carcinoma.
Treatment with any other investigational agents for this condition.
Major surgical procedure (excluding vascular access device placement) or significant trauma within 28 days of enrollment or expected need for major surgery during the study.
Extracranial palliative radiotherapy within 7 days prior to enrollment.
Known hypersensitivity to trastuzumab, perturbzumab, paclitaxel, nab-paclitaxel, docetaxel, trastuzumab deruxtecan, ado‑trastuzumab emtansine, or tucatinib.
Myocardial infarction within 6 months preceding enrollment.
Symptomatic congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association class II–IV).
Any untreated intracranial lesion on screening magnetic resonance imaging unless sponsor approval obtained.
Use of systemic glucocorticoids exceeding 2 mg dexamethasone equivalents daily for management of brain metastases.
Confirmed leptomeningeal disease on screening imaging.
Frequent (> once weekly) generalized or complex partial seizures inadequately controlled.
Clinically significant arrhythmias requiring intervention (grade 3 or higher) or uncontrolled atrial fibrillation.
History of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis necessitating steroids, existing ILD/pneumonitis, or unresolved suspicious findings on imaging.
Pulmonary co‑morbidities such as recent pulmonary embolism (within 3 months), severe asthma, moderate-to-severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, or substantial pleural effusions.
Active autoimmune, connective‑tissue, or inflammatory disorders involving the lungs (e.g., rheumatoid arthritis, Sjögren’s syndrome, sarcoidosis).
Previous pneumonectomy.
Active or uncontrolled serious infection.
Incapacity to ingest oral tablets or gastrointestinal pathology impairing drug absorption.
Grade 2 or greater diarrhea of any cause persisting at screening.
Concomitant administration of potent cytochrome P450 2C8 or 3A4 enzyme inhibitors or inducers.
Pregnancy or lactation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia (lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma secreting IgM). Cancer burden allowed: symptomatic disease meeting consensus treatment indication criteria (requiring therapy). Prior treatment required: anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody‑containing regimen **and** Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor **and** overall ≥ two distinct prior lines of therapy. Prior treatment excluded: any CD19‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: serum immunoglobulin M concentration > 2 times the upper limit of normal. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of central nervous system lymphoma; pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; active human immunodeficiency virus infection; chronic hepatitis B or C requiring active treatment; unstable angina or myocardial infarction within the preceding six months; NYHA class III or IV congestive heart failure; uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias; any Grade 3 or 4 cardiac disease; history of Stevens‑Johnson syndrome or toxic epidermal necrolysis; concurrent systemic immunosuppressants; administration of live, attenuated vaccines within four weeks of dosing; recent untreated infection requiring systemic therapy within fourteen days; major surgery within four weeks of dosing; clinically significant third‑space fluid accumulations (ascites or pleural effusion) unless confirmed malignant by cytology; ongoing alcohol or illicit drug dependence; prior non‑lymphoma malignancy except appropriately treated low‑risk skin cancers, carcinoma in situ, early-stage bladder or prostate cancer, or other solid tumors in complete remission; persistent adverse events greater than Grade 1 attributable to earlier therapy (except alopecia any grade and peripheral neuropathy up to Grade 2 permissible); clinically significant preexisting liver disease including cirrhosis or viral/autoinflammatory hepatitis; inadequate organ or marrow reserve as defined by absolute neutrophils < 1000/mm³, platelets < 50,000/mm³, hemoglobin < 7 g/dL, bilirubin ≤ 1.5 × ULN (up to 3 × ULN acceptable with certain conditions), transaminases ≤ 2.5 × ULN (up to 5 × ULN allowable with hepatic comorbidity), creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min; inability or unwillingness to comply with study procedures.

general_exclusion_criteria:

Bulky cervical adenopathy compressing the upper airway.
Major surgery or significant traumatic injury within four weeks of study start, or incomplete recovery from such events.
Active hepatitis C infection (presence of HCV antibody) unless HCV RNA is undetectable.
Positive hepatitis B surface antigen or core antibody with detectable HBV DNA; patients with undetectable HBV DNA may enter only with prophylactic antiviral therapy.
Ongoing active bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic, or other infections necessitating antimicrobial therapy at enrollment.
Confirmed active SARS‑CoV‑2 infection.
History of another malignancy (other than non‑melanoma skin cancer, in‑situ cervical or breast carcinoma, or Gleason 6 prostate cancer observed) unless disease‑free ≥2 years or deemed low risk by investigator.
Recent receipt of live attenuated vaccinations (within seven days) or administration within twenty‑eight days of first study dose.

Known central nervous system involvement by lymphoma.
Peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 1.

Use of any investigational drug within four weeks prior to first dose.
Chemotherapy or other non‑investigational anticancer therapies (aside from CD20 monoclonal antibodies) administered within four weeks prior to first dose.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intent to conceive during the study.
Previous solid organ transplantation or allogenic stem cell transplantation.
Documented or presumed haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.

History of severe inherited bleeding disorders (hemophilia A/B, Von Wieberburg disease) or acquired coagulopathies demanding regular warfarin therapy.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of active or untreated CNS metastases or leptomeningeal disease; uncontrolled symptomatic visceral disease (massive effusion, >50% hepatic involvement, pulmonary lymphangitic spread); prior chemotherapy or elacestrant in the metastatic setting; known germline BRCA mutation without prior PARP inhibitor; concurrent malignant disease within 3 years (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, cervical CIS); unresolved acute serious infections; documented pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; major surgery within 28 days; inability to swallow oral meds or clinically significant GI disorders affecting absorption; hypersensitivity to elacestrant or excipients; pregnancy, breastfeeding, or inadequate contraceptive measures; current substance/alcohol dependence; unacceptable cardiovascular, hematologic, hepatic, or renal impairment; ECOG performance status ≥2.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Diagnosed with acute promyelocytic leukemia.
Active central nervous system leukemia.
Allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant within 60 days of first dose or clinically significant graft‑versus‑host disease requiring escalations of immunosuppression.
Chronic myeloid leukemia.
History of untreated or progressive acute or chronic toxicity from prior anticancer therapy (grade > 1).
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to any component of emavusertib.
Concurrent active advanced malignant solid tumour.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection or AIDS‑defining illness.
Active hepatitis B virus DNA positivity or hepatitis C virus infection less than 6 months old unless viral load undetectable, and hepatitis C with cirrhosis.
Uncontrolled or severe cardiovascular disease—including myocardial infarction or unstable angina within 6 months, New York Heart Association class II or greater congestive heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction < 50 %, serious arrhythmias, clinically significant pericardial disease, cardiac amyloidosis, congenital long QT syndrome, or corrected QT interval > 450 msec.
Clinically significant gastrointestinal disease impairing swallowing, oral absorption, or tolerability of oral medication.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Systemic fungal, bacterial, viral, or other infection that is not adequately controlled.
Other severe, acute, or chronic medical, psychiatric, or social conditions, or laboratory abnormalities that would increase risk of trial participation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: any non‑small cell lung cancer histology. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease (locally advanced/unresectable or distant metastases) with measurable disease per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: at least one prior systemic antitumour therapy and no more than three lines of systemic therapy in the advanced/metastatic setting, with disease progression following the most recent therapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior KRAS G12C inhibitor or pan‑KRAS/RAS inhibitor. Biomarkers required: KRAS G12C mutation detected in tumour tissue (assessed on archived formalin‑fixed, paraffin‑embedded specimen). Biomarkers excluded: presence of any other oncogenic driver alteration (e.g., epidermal growth factor receptor mutation, Anaplastic lymphoma kinase rearrangement, ROS1 rearrangement).
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known hypersensitivity to any component of divarasib, sotorasib, or adagrasib.
Malabsorption syndrome or other condition that would interfere with enteral absorption.
Presence of a known concomitant second oncogenic driver.
Mixed small‑cell lung cancer or large‑cell neuroendocrine histology.
Active or untreated central nervous system metastases.
Leptomeningeal disease or carcinomatous meningitis.
Uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites requiring recurrent drainage procedures twice weekly or more frequently.
Any infection that could impact patient safety.
More than 30 Gray of thoracic radiotherapy administered within six months before randomisation.
Uncontrolled tumour‑related pain.
Unresolved toxicity from prior anticancer therapy.
History of another malignant neoplasm within five years preceding screening, except for adequately treated carcinoma in situ of the cervix, non‑melanoma skin cancer, localized prostate cancer, ductal carcinoma in situ, or stage I uterine cancer.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Non small cell lung cancer. Prior treatment excluded: Prior adoptive cell therapy, prior gene therapy, prior allogeneic stem cell transplantation, prior solid organ transplantation. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another primary malignant neoplasm within three years prior to enrolment (exceptions permitted for non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix/bladder/breast, or low‑grade prostate cancer).
Active untreated primary central nervous system malignancy.
Stroke or transient ischemic attack occurring within twelve months preceding enrolment.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease within six months prior to enrolment or any episode of congestive heart failure at any time.
Severe acute hypersensitivity reaction to cyclophosphamide, fludarabine, recombinant interleukin‑2, methotrexate, gentamicin, or other aminoglycoside antibiotics.
Primary immunodeficiency disorders.
Receipt of a live attenuated vaccine within four weeks prior to enrolment or intention to receive a live vaccine before lymphodepletion and investigational product infusion.
Ongoing autoimmune or inflammatory condition necessitating chronic systemic corticosteroids or other immunosuppressants (aside from steroid taper following prior checkpoint inhibition).
Female participants of reproductive potential who are currently pregnant, nursing, or planning pregnancy during the study period.
Documented diagnosis of Li‑Fraumeni syndrome in the participant or close family members.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: primary central nervous system diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior chemotherapy or radiation therapy directed to primary CNS lymphoma; any prior anti‑Programmed Death‑1, anti‑Programmed Death‑Ligand 1, anti‑Programmed Death‑Ligand 2, anti‑Cytotoxic T‑Ly​mpho­cit­­e‑Associated Protein 4, OX‑40, or CD137 targeted agents. Biomarkers required: HIV‐negative serology. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants who cannot undergo magnetic resonance imaging.
Intraocular primary CNS lymphoma without accompanying brain or spinal cord disease.
Receipt of any other investigational agents concurrently.
History of Grade ≥3 allergic or hypersensitivity reaction to pembrolizumab, high‑dose methotrexate, temozolomide, rituximab, or their excipients.
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment (excluding physiological hormone replacements) within the past 2 years.
Live vaccinations administered within 30 days preceding the first dose of study drug.
Presence of a second actively progressing malignancy or requiring active treatment within the past 3 years (exceptions noted for cured superficial skin cancers).
Past or current non‑infectious pneumonitis necessitating steroids.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus with glycated haemoglobin > 8%.
Unable to ingest oral medication or significant gastrointestinal impairment (e.g., malabsorption, gastric/small‑bowel resections, complete intestinal obstruction).
Concurrent use of moderate or strong CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers (including enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants) that cannot be safely stopped.
Known active hepatitis B infection (positive HBsAg) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA).
Active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
Immunodeficient conditions or chronic systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents.
Previous allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Large pleural effusions, ascites, or generalized oedema.
Significant untreated psychiatric illness or substance misuse impairing protocol compliance.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child during the study period extending to 120 days after the last dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive carcinoma (ductal or lobular). Cancer burden allowed: American Joint Committee on Cancer stage II–III disease with no evidence of recurrent local or distant disease; mandatory presence of disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow identified by immunohistochemistry. Prior treatment required: Completed definitive surgery plus any indicated chemotherapy and radiation; currently receiving adjuvant endocrine therapy (aromatase inhibitor preferred; tamoxifen allowable if planned conversion to aromatase inhibitor ≥ 21 days before initiating hydroxychloroquine-containing regimen); may have received a prior cyclin-dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor **other than** palbociclib. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior exposure to palbociclib; simultaneous enrollement in another interventional trial; adjuvant endocrine therapies other than aromatic­oil‑inducing agents or appropriately converted tamoxifen; investigational anticancer agents. Biomarkers required: Estrogen‑receptor‑positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2‑negative status on the primary tumor; detectable disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow by centralized immunohistochemical assay. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of another prior invasive breast cancer (except ductal carcinoma in situ diagnosed > 5 years ago); prior invasive non‑breast malignancy unless disease‑free ≥ 5 years; chronic high‑dose systemic steroid use (>specified thresholds) or other immunosuppressive agents; baseline ECG showing QTc > 480 ms; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease—including clinically significant untreated arrhythmias (atrial fibrillation allowed if rate‑controlled), acute coronary syndrome, coronary intervention, or stent placement within ≤ 6 months, or New York Heart Association class II or higher heart failure; past or existing pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease, diffusing capacity of carbon monoxide < 50 % predicted, or resting arterial oxygen saturation ≤ 88 %; unmanaged diabetes mellitus; active, serious bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection; moderate-to-severe liver disease such as cirrhosis or chronic active/hepatitis C/B; HIV infection requiring antiretroviral therapy; gastrointestinal diseases markedly impairing oral drug absorption (e.g., inflammatory bowel disease flare, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, extensive resections); active bleeding disorder or therapeutic anticoagulation; historic or current retinopathy or retinal vascular occlusion; pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraceptive measures in persons of reproductive potential.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: squamous. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic (stage IV) disease without symptomatic central nervous system metastases, no leptomeningeal disease, and no centrally located cerebral lesions measuring ≥1.5 cm that cause symptoms. Prior treatment required: none. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy for metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. Biomarkers required: programmed death ligand‑1 tumour proportion score >50% determined by 22C3 immunohistochemistry (assessment mandated during screening). Biomarkers excluded: presence of any known actionable genomic alteration (e.g., epidermal growth factor receptor sensitising mutation, anaplastic lymphoma kinase rearrangement, ros proto‑oncogene 1 rearrangement, BRAF V600E mutation, mesenchymal–epithelial transition factor exon 14 skipping, ret proto‑oncogene fusion, neurotrophic tropomyosin receptor kinases fusion).
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease requiring systemic steroids. Active autoimmune disease or chronic inflammatory lung condition necessitating systemic therapy. Peripheral neuropathy graded ≥2. Recent severe infection within four weeks preceding randomization. Major surgical procedure or significant traumatic injury within four weeks preceding randomization. Ongoing participation in another therapeutic investigational study. Previous malignant neoplasm (other than adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer or cervical carcinoma in situ) unless disease‐free for three years. Uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions including congestive heart failure. Significant hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh class C) or bilirubin elevation greater than twice ULN. Renal insufficiency with creatinine clearance less than thirty milliliters per minute. Human immunodeficiency virus infection, active hepatitis B or C infection. Poor functional status exceeding ECOG Performance Status 1.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of allergy to tumor‑necrosis factor alpha, lomustine, excipients, or intravenous human proteins/peptides/antibodies.
Absolute neutrophil count <1.5 × 10⁹/L, platelets <100 × 10⁹/L, hemoglobin <9.0 g/dL.
Creatinine clearance <60 mL/min or serum creatinine >1.5 times upper limit of normal.
Alanine aminotransferase, aspartate aminotransferase, alkaline phosphatase ≥2.5 times upper limit of normal or total bilirubin ≥2.0 times upper limit of normal.
International normalized ratio >1.5 upper limit of normal.
Inability to undergo contrast‑enhanced magnetic resonance imaging.
Active or past autoimmune disease that could worsen with immune stimulation.
Recent (<1 year) cerebrovascular event or acute/subacute coronary syndrome, heart failure NYHA class III–IV, serious cardiac arrhythmia, left ventricular ejection fraction <55%, uncontrolled hypertension, arterial aneurysm at high risk of rupture, ischemic peripheral vascular disease grade IIb‑IV.
Significant psychiatric illness (major depression, bipolar I/II, OCD, schizophrenia, suicide attempts/ideas, homicide ideas) or anxiety ≥CTCAE grade 3.
Severe diabetic retinopathy (severe non‑proliferative or proliferative).
Major trauma or major surgery within 4 weeks preceding treatment.
Known history of tuberculosis.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Requirement for chronic high‑dose corticosteroids (>4 mg/day dexamethasone equivalents) or other immunosuppressants; must be off or on stable/decreasing dose ≤4 mg/day dexamethasone for ≥7 days pre‑treatment.
Active/uncontrolled infection or other severe comorbidity posing undue risk.
Concurrent malignancy unless disease‑free ≥2 years without intervention.
Use of growth factors or immunomodulatory agents within 7 days prior to study treatment.
Serious non‑healing wounds, ulcers, or bone fractures.
Anticoagulation with P2Y12 inhibitors or vitamin K antagonists.
Concomitant anticancer therapies apart from investigational product.
Live vaccinations within 4 weeks prior to treatment or planned during study.

general_exclusion_criteria:

* Greater than Grade 2 intracranular hemorrhage.
* Active systemic disease.

* Recent systemic antitumour therapy or radiotherapy administered within four weeks (excluded as washout periods are disregarded).
* Significant cardiovascular disease such as symptomatic arrhythmia, congestive heart failure, myocardial infarction within six months, NYHA class III/IV, or QTc > 480 ms (excluding well‑controlled asymptomatic atrial fibrillation).
* Difficulty swallowing oral medications or serious gastrointestinal disorder affecting drug absorption.
* Current human immunodeficiency virus infection, past progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy, or any active severe infection (bacterial, viral, fungal).
* Hypersensitivity or allergic reactions to acalabrutinib or its ingredients.
* Active bleeding disorders or history of bleeding diatheses (e.g., hemophilia, Von Willebrand disease).
* Uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anaemia or immune thrombocytopaenia.
* Requirement for potent CYP3A4 enzyme modulators (strong inhibitors or inducers).
* Ongoing anticoagulation with warfarin or comparable vitamin K antagonist.
* Coagulation parameters PT/INR or aPTT greater than twice the upper limit of normal (absent lupus anticoagulant).
* Cerebrovascular accident or intracerebral bleed occurring within six months preceding first dose.
* Major surgical operation conducted within twenty‑eight days before first dose.
* Positive hepatitis B core antibody with detectable HBV DNA, or positivity for hepatitis B surface antigen, or hepatitis C RNA positivity.
* Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of acceptable contraceptive method for women of child‑bearing potential.
* Participation concurrently in another therapeutic clinical trial.
* Advanced hepatic impairment classified as Child‑Pugh score C.
* Untreated or poorly managed arterial hypertension.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of significant cardiovascular disease (symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious arrhythmia, uncontrolled hypertension, recent myocardial infarction, stroke/TIA ≤ 6 months, recent thromboembolism ≤ 1 month).
Active gastrointestinal disorders placing patient at high risk for perforation or fistula (e.g., active IBD, diverticulitis, cholecystitis, cholangitis, appendicitis, pancreatitis, obstructive jaundice, gastric outlet obstruction, intra‑abdominal abscess ≤ 3 months).
Clinically significant unresolved bleeding episodes (hematuria, hematemesis, hemoptysis, pulmonary hemorrhage) within 3 months.
Lesion invasion of major pulmonary vascular structures.
Uncompensated or symptomatic endocrine abnormalities (hypothyroidism requiring replacement, hypercalcaemia/phosphataemia refractory to correction).
Severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh class B or C) or requirement for regular dialysis.
Solid organ transplantation recipients.
Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inducers/inhibitors within prohibited windows.
Therapeutic anticoagulation with coumadin, direct thrombin inhibitors, direct Factor Xa inhibitors (betrixaban) or platelet aggregation blockers (clopidogrel) (except prophylactic low‑dose aspirin or low‑molecular‑weight heparin as stipulated).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding; females of child‑bearing potential not employing adequate contraception or lacking a negative pregnancy test ≤ 28 days.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Systemic AL amyloidosis (light chain amyloidosis). Histology allowed: Immunoglobulin light chain deposition disease. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after at least one prior line of therapy (minimum two cycles); progressive light chain disease defined as differential free light chain >20 mg/L; cardiac stage I, II, or IIIa according to the 2013 European modification of Mayo Clinic staging (Stage IIIB excluded). Prior treatment required: Prior therapy consisting of daratumumab together with cyclophosphamide, bortezomib and dexamethasone (darum​atumumab + CyBorD). Prior treatment excluded: Prior BCMA‑targeting bispecific antibody therapy; prior BCMA‑directed chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy; refractory to belantamab mafodotin; belantamab mafodotin received as the immediate prior line of therapy. Biomarkers required: Differential free light chain >20 mg/L (assessed at screening); for phase 2 enrolment, fulfillment of ANY ONE of the following laboratory criteria assessed at screening: difference between involved and uninvolved free light chain >40 mg/L **or** abnormal free light chain level with abnormal κ/λ ratio (exceptions apply for CKD stage ≥3) **or** serum M‑protein spike ≥0.5 g/dL. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of allergic reaction to elranatamab.; Ongoing investigation with any other experimental drug for AL amyloidosis.; Active concurrent malignancies except adequately treated skin carcinomas, in‑situ cervical cancer, stage I solid tumors in remission >2 years, or low‑risk prostate cancer (Gleason < 7, PSA < 10 ng/mL).; Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive during study and up to four months post‑treatment.; Requirement for effective contraception not met.; Unresolved toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia).; Active plasma cell myeloma fulfilling CRAB criteria (aside from allowable free light chain ratios).; Clinically significant untreated autoimmune disorders.; Positive testing for Human Immunodeficiency Virus.; Significant hepatic impairment, renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min), or uncontrolled cardiovascular disease not outlined in cardiac staging criteria.; Orthostatic hypotension causing syncope unresponsive to optimal therapy.; Planned autologous stem‑cell transplantation within six months of starting study medication.; Recent (<3 months) acute coronary syndrome or unstable ventricular arrhythmias.; Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 30%; severe valvular stenosis or serious congenital heart defects.; Documented sustained ventricular tachyarrhythmia or life‑threatening bradyarrhythmia without appropriate pacing devices.; Corrected QT interval >550 ms on screening electrocardiogram absent a paced rhythm.; Electrocardiographic signs of acute myocardial ischemia or prohibited conduction disturbances (other than isolated first‑degree AV block, Mobitz Type Ⅰ second‑degree AV block, right or left bundle‑branch blocks, controlled atrial fibrillation, or stable bifascicular block deemed benign).; Major surgical procedure requiring general anaesthesia within four weeks before registration or anticipated during the study.; New York Heart Association class IV heart failure or acutely decompensated congestion.; Eligibility restricted for transplant‑candidate individuals who have not yet proceeded to transplant.

Other systemic immunosuppressive medication within 30 days of randomisation.
Active peptic ulcer disease, inflammatory bowel disease, ulcerative colitis, recent gastrointestinal perforation, abdominal fistula, or intra‑abdominal abscess within 4 weeks.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Pain due to metastatic prostate cancer requiring opioid or other analgesics.
ECOG performance status ≥3.
Requires chronic urinary self‑catheterisation because of bladder outlet obstruction (indwelling Foley/suprapubic catheter acceptable).
Evidence of visceral involvement posing undue risk with high‑dose testosterone (e.g., femoral metastasis risking pathologic fracture, extensive spine metastasis threatening cord compression, bulky hepatic metastases).
Serious or unstable pre‑existing medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormality compromising safety or consent.
Active uncontrolled infection, including known HIV/AIDS or hepatitis B/C.
Inability to comprehend or voluntarily sign informed consent.
Concurrent therapeutic anticoagulation with warfarin, rivaroxaban, or apixaban (unless switched to low molecular weight heparin before study entry).
History of thromboembolic event within past 12 months not managed with ongoing systemic anticoagulation.
Hematrocrit >51%; untreated severe obstructive sleep apnea; uncontrolled or severely compromised congestive heart failure.
Allergy to sesame seed oil or cottonseed oil.
Major surgery requiring general anaesthesia performed within 3 weeks preceding screening or incomplete postoperative healing.

Inability to understand or sign informed consent documents.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Endometrial intraepithelial neoplasia (also accepted terminology: complex atypical hyperplasia, atypical hyperplasia). Histology allowed: Premalignant endometrial glandular epithelium consistent with EIN. Cancer burden allowed: Non‑invasive disease confined to the uterus (premalignant lesion, no stromal/myoinvasion). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Current use of hormonal contraceptives or systemic/post‑menopausal hormone replacement therapy involving progestins exceeding low‑dose limits; current use of medroxyprogesterone acetate >10 mg/day, norethisterone/norethistrone acetate >10 mg/day, oral micronised progesterone >300 mg/day, or any progestin regimen continued >8 weeks prior to enrolment; current use of selective estrogen receptor modulator; current use of aromatase inhibitor; current use of metformin; current use of GLP‑1 or dual GLP‑1/GIP‑1 receptor agonist; receipt of any investigational agent within 30 days of enrollment; use of dofetilide, ulipristal, carbonic anhydrase inhibitor, ranolazine, vandetanib, dolutegravir, or cimetidine; use of sulfonylureas or meglitinides in diabetic subjects; excessive alcohol consumption/alcohol use disorder. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pulmonary embolism, thrombotic stroke, arterial thrombosis, or deep vein thrombosis.
Pregnancy or planning to become pregnant during the study period.
Breastfeeding.
Uncontrolled intercurrent medical or psychiatric illness limiting protocol adherence.
Active infectious diseases such as untreated HIV, hepatitis B/C with detectable viral loads.
Significant organ dysfunction not meeting laboratory inclusion cutoffs (severe hepatic, renal, or cardiovascular insufficiency).
Inability to give informed consent.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥65 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior CDK4/6 inhibitor for metastatic breast cancer; prior CDK4/6 inhibitor administered within the preceding 12 months in the neoadjuvant or adjuvant setting; prior exposure to the planned endocrine‐therapy partner (aromatase inhibitor or fulvestrant) lasting more than 30 consecutive days. Biomarkers required: Hormone‑receptor positive (estrogen receptor–positive and/or progesterone‑receptor positive) and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Rapidly progressive brain metastases.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness that would impede compliance with study procedures.
Concurrent therapy with other investigational agents.
Use of concomitant medicines known to markedly prolong the QT interval.
Presence of another actively treated malignancy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced unresectable disease or distant metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: receipt of an antibody‑drug conjugate directed against HER2 and/or systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior radiopharmaceutical therapy (including radioembolisation). Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative and somatostatin‑receptor positivity documented by SSTR‑PET imaging (minimum tumour SUVmax exceeding liver SUVmean in at least eighty percent of RECIST v1.1 measurable lesions). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Significant cardiovascular disease.
Active or symptomatic central nervous system metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, spinal cord compression, or leptomeningeal disease.
Unresolved toxicities from prior treatments graded higher than CTCAE Grade 1 (except alopecia).
History of severe allergic reaction to actinium‑225, gallium‑68, copper‑64, octreotate, or any excipient of DOTATATE imaging agents.
Concurrent or recent malignant neoplasm judged to potentially affect study outcomes.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 12-49 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Ewing sarcoma. Histology allowed: Ewing sarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: RECIST measurable disease; no central nervous system metastasis. Prior treatment required: Relapsed or refractory disease after standard therapy; may have received prior irinotecan, liposomal irinotecan, and/or temozolomide. Prior treatment excluded: Progression while receiving combined irinotecan plus temozolomide; prior exposure to PEEL-224. Biomarkers required: Documented FET‑ETS family translocation (evidence of any of EWSR1::FLI1, EWSR1::ERG, EWSR1::ETV1, EWSR1::ETV4, EWSR1::FEV, FUS::FLI1, FUS::ERG) confirmed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Concurrent receipt of any additional antitumour agents for the current sarcoma indication.  
* Administration of strong CYP1A2 or CYP3A4 enzyme inhibitors or inducers within 14 days of the first PEEL-224 dose (except levofloxacin, which is permitted).  
* History of solid organ transplantation or allogeneic haematopoietic stem cell transplantation.  
* Pregnancy or breastfeeding.  
* Known hypersensitivity to polyethylene glycol‑containing products, camptothecin derivatives, temozolomide, or vincristine.  
* Uncontrolled comorbidities including active infections, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, or severe psychiatric disorders limiting adherence to study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: Acute myeloid leukemia (excluding acute promyelocytic leukemia). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed disease documented by ≥5 % myeloblasts in bone marrow or return of blasts in peripheral blood, **or** refractory disease after ≤2 prior induction regimens; Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status ≤2. Prior treatment required: Conventional induction chemotherapy resulting in relapse or refractory disease. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Subjects with known acute promyelocytic leukemia.; Active central nervous system involvement with acute myeloid leukemia.; Less than 100 days elapsed since allogeneic hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation.; Current active graft‑versus‑host disease.; Unresolved adverse events greater than Grade 1 attributable to prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia).; Concurrent participation in another investigational study.; Prohibited concomitant medications (cannot take gliptins; strong CYP3A4 inducers or inhibitors without adjustment.).; History of orthostatic hypotension with systolic BP < 100 mmHg or uncontrolled hypertension.; New York Heart Association class II or higher heart failure.; Presence of another active malignancy within the preceding 2 years, except adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, ductal carcinoma in situ of the breast, or low‑grade prostate cancer.; Active untreated hepatitis B virus infection (eligible only if suppressed with undetectable DNA); Active untreated hepatitis C virus infection (eligible only if cleared or virologically suppressed).; Pregnancy or current breastfeeding.; Significant psychiatric disorder or social circumstances limiting adherence to study procedures.; Ongoing serious uncontrolled medical conditions such as active infection despite antimicrobial therapy, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia, or other intercurrent illness judged by investigators to compromise safety or data interpretation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Myelodysplastic syndrome. Histology allowed: Myelodysplastic syndrome with excess blasts‑2. Cancer burden allowed: Bone‐marrow blast count 10–19 % or peripheral blood blasts 5–19 % or presence of Auer rods, together with disease that is refractory or relapsed after at least four cycles of a hypomethylating agent (azacitidine, decitabine, or oral azacitidine/cedazuridine); Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status ≤2. Prior treatment required: Minimum of four cycles of a hypomethylating agent. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Female or Male. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease (unresectable locally advanced or distant); previously treated brain metastases permitted, untreated or symptomatic brain metastases not permitted. Prior treatment required: Received a taxane **AND** received trastuzumab **AND** received trastuzumab deruxtecan. Prior treatment excluded: Prior therapy with any Trop‑2‑directed antibody‑drug conjugate (including sacituzumab govitecin). Biomarkers required: HER2 positive (per ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe allergic reaction or hypersensitivity to trastuzumab, sacituzumab govitecan, or their components; known UGT1A1 ∗28/*28 genotype; untreated, symptomatic, or steroid‐requiring brain metastases; leptomeningeal disease; major surgery within two weeks preceding study entry; concurrent serious medical disorders (active infections, uncontrolled cardiovascular, pulmonary, hepatic, or renal disease, uncontrolled diabetes, seizure disorder); current pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability to comply with contraception requirements; inadequate organ or bone‑marrow function outside protocol specifications; performance status worse than ECOG 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: high‑risk oral precancerous disease. Histology allowed: proliferative leukoplakia. Cancer burden allowed: oral cavity lesion with at least one injectable measurable lesion ≥1 cm; no evidence of head and neck cancer recurrence within the preceding 3 months. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Prior treatment with an oncolytic virus therapy.  
• Current systemic infection requiring intravenous antibiotics.  
• Chronic use (>4 weeks) of systemic antivirals having anti‑herpetic activity (e.g., acyclovir, valganciclovir).  
• Active severe herpetic infection or prior serious complication of HSV‑1 infection (except occasional asymptomatic cold sore).  
• Positive hepatitis B surface antigen or detectable hepatitis C viral RNA (effective treatment permits enrolment).  
• Documented HIV infection.  
• History of stage III or IV invasive head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.  
• Long‑term corticosteroid use (>4 weeks) at doses greater than prednisolone 20 mg/day (or equivalent).  
• Personal history of hematopoietic stem cell or solid organ transplantation.  
• Presence of another active malignancy, except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, low‑risk prostate adenocarcinoma on active surveillance, or cancers in remission ≥2 years.  
• Clinically significant bleeding episode within the past 6 months posing increased procedural hemorrhage risk.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Hormone receptor positive and Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 negative (HR+/HER2‑) endocrine resistant/refractory. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent, unresectable, or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: 1 to 3 prior lines of non‑endocrine‑based systemic therapy for advanced disease. Prior treatment excluded: Antibody‑drug conjugates containing monomethylauristatin E (MMAE) payload. Biomarkers required: Nectin4 gene amplification demonstrated by a clinically validated assay. Biomarkers excluded: HER2‑positive (immunohistochemistry 3+ or in situ hybridization positive).
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known hypersensitivity or allergy to any ingredient of the investigational product or to MMAE.
Active keratitis or corneal ulcerations.
Active or untreated central nervous system metastases.
Uncontrolled diabetes or hypertension.
Uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites requiring recurring drainage procedures (monthly or more frequently).
Active interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring corticosteroids greater than 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other immunosuppressants.
Requirement for concurrent administration of strong cyclodextrin 3A enzyme inhibitors/inducers or P‑glycoprotein inhibitors during the study.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: clear cell carcinoma (≥50% clear cell component). Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or persistent disease with at least one meas­urable lesion according to RECIST v1.1 (stable brain metastases permissible). Prior treatment required: at least one prior platinum‑based chemotherapy regimen. Prior treatment excluded: prior belzutifan or any other hypoxia‑inducible factor‑2α inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pulse oximetry < 92% at rest; requiring continuous or intermittent supplemental oxygen; anti‑cancer therapy (chemo/radiation/other investigational) within four weeks of enrolment (six weeks for nitrosoureas or mitomycin C); prior small‑molecule kinase inhibitor within two weeks; radiation therapy within two weeks without recovery from toxicity; use of prohibited herbal supplements; concomitant moderate or strong CYP3A4 inducers; participation in another investigational study within four weeks; untreated grade > 1 adverse events from prior therapy (excluding alopecia, hormonally managed endocrinopathies, or grade 2 neuropathy); administration of live or live‑attenuated vaccine within thirty days; major surgery within four weeks or minor surgery (aside from ports) within one week; gastrointestinal disorders preventing oral intake or dependence on feeding tubes/parenteral nutrition; inability to swallow oral medication; significant cardiovascular disease (unstabilised hypertension, NYHA Class II–III heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction < six months ago, serious arrhythmias < six months ago); moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh B or C); granulocyte‑colony stimulating factors, GM‑CSF, or epoetin alfa within twenty‑eight days; diagnosed immunodeficiency; HIV infection; active hepatitis B or C infection (treated/cured HCV acceptable); uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses—including active infections, non‑healing wounds or ulcers; psychiatric or social circumstances limiting protocol adherence; hypersensitivity to belzutifan or structurally similar compounds; pregnancy; lactation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≤75 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: Plasma cell neoplasm (multiple myeloma). Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed, measurable disease (Serum M protein ≥0.5 g/dL **or** Urine M protein ≥200 mg/24 h **or** Involved serum free light chain ≥10 mg/dL **and** Abnormal serum free light‐chain ratio <0.26 or >1.65). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy for multiple myeloma. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another malignant disease within 3 years (excluding fully excised basal or squamous cell skin cancer, in situ lesions, or cured low‑risk prostate cancer).
Central nervous system involvement.
Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 3, or grade 2 with pain on exam.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions (uncontrolled hypertension, arrhythmia, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, grade 3 thromboembolism, myocardial infarction within 6 months).
Major surgical procedure or radiation therapy within 4 weeks prior to starting protocol therapy.
Daily systemic corticosteroid use equivalent to >10 mg prednisolone for >7 days.
Concurrent symptomatic AL amyloidosis or plasma cell leukemia.
POEMS syndrome.
Active infection requiring antimicrobial therapy within 14 days of study start.

general_exclusion_criteria: Active hepatitis B or C infection. Human immunodeficiency virus infection not meeting strict immune‐function criteria. Uncontrolled concurrent malignancy requiring active therapy (except selected low‑risk skin cancers and appropriately treated localized prostate cancer). Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or inability/willfulness to comply with contraception and REMS requirements. Ongoing active infectious processes needing intravenous antimicrobial therapy or recent major infection. Significant preexisting liver disease compromising safe participation. Active central nervous system involvement by lymphoma. Grade 2 or higher adverse effects attributable to prior cancer therapies. Severe allergic or anaphylactic reaction histories to monoclonal antibodies or related biologics. Confirmed progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy. Hypersensitivity to immunomodulatory drugs or formulation components. High risk for thromboembolic events without willingness to accept prophylaxis. Requirement for ongoing anticoagulation incompatible with study medication. Recent live vaccination (within 28 days). Persistent substantial cardiopulmonary comorbidity (NYHA class III–IV heart failure, recent MI, unstable arrhythmias, severe COPD or asthma exacerbation). Poor functional status (ECOG >2) or inadequate organ reserve outside stipulated laboratory thresholds.

general_exclusion_criteria: Treatment with any investigational product or other anticancer therapy within 14 days or five half‐lives of first dose. Deleterious PTEN mutation or absence of PTEN protein expression by immunohistochemistry. Recent major surgery or extensive radiotherapy (within 28 days) or limited palliative radiotherapy (within 7 days). Active untreated central nervous system metastases. Systemic glucocorticoid use exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents at enrollment. Uncontrolled type 1 or type 2 diabetes mellitus. Genetic metabolic disorders such as Crigler‑Najjar syndrome or Gilbert’s syndrome. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Concurrent active malignancy or prior malignant disease diagnosed within the past 2 years. Clinically significant cardiovascular disease—including myocardial infarction or acute coronary syndrome within 6 months, symptomatic congestive heart failure grade ≥ 2, serious arrhythmias or conduction abnormalities within 6 months, or uncontrolled hypertension. Conditions interfering with oral medication intake or absorption. History of drug‑induced pneumonitis. HIV infection with CD4 < 350 cells/µL, recent opportunistic infections, or inadequate antiretroviral control. Positive hepatitis B core antibody without protective serologic profile or detectable HBV DNA; likewise, positive hepatitis C antibody without undetectable HCV RNA after cure. Congenital long QT syndrome or QTcF prolongation >470 msec on screening electrocardiogram. Current use of proton‑pump inhibitors, potent UGT2B7 modifiers, or sensitive substrate medicines transported by OAT1, OAT3, BCRP, or OATP1B1 where interaction risk exists.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Stem cell transplantation within 12 weeks prior to enrolment or active graft‑versus‑host disease.
Active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, SARS‑CoV‑2, HIV, or any untreated bacterial, fungal, or viral infection.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease occurring within 6 months (acute MI, unstable angina, severe arrhythmia, thromboembolism, stroke, prolonged QTc >470 ms, etc.).
Any other active malignant tumour diagnosed within the last 3 years except adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, or carcinoma in situ.
Grade ≥2 peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy; history of Guillain‑Barré syndrome or Grade ≥3 peripheral motor polyneuropathy.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate contraceptive measures.
